The foreword highlights that "in the 2021 Edition of Public Service Rules (PSR), emerging issues such as performance management systems, talent sourcing, volunteerism, and virtual meetings/engagements, which are products of our recent experience in the management of the COVID-19 pandemic, and many others are reflected."

questions.question: Which PSR term means temporary removal from normal duties during dismissal proceedings?
questions.explanation: Interdiction means temporary removal from normal duties during dismissal proceedings, usually on half salary pending the outcome.

questions.question: According to the Public Service Rules, who is responsible for making appointments into posts on GL. 06 and below?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The respective Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office). Rule 020103(a) states that each ministry/extra-ministerial office must handle all appointments to junior posts on GL. 06 and below.

questions.question: Which option aligns with the meaning of the workflow for terminating a non-pensionable appointment?
options: The engagement may be terminated by giving one month's notice or paying one month's salary in lieu of notice.
The engagement may be terminated by paying three months' salary. a
The engagement may be terminated without notice.
The engagement may be terminated at the discretion of the Permanent Secretary.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The engagement may be terminated by giving one month's notice or paying one month's salary in lieu of notice.). Rule 021210 states that the engagement "may at any time terminate the engagement of the person engaged by giving three months' notice in writing or by paying one month's salary in lieu of notice.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer who is absent from duty without leave for 14 consecutive days may be deemed by the service to have?
questions.explanation: Rule 030413 states that unauthorized absence for 14 consecutive days is construed as resignation from the service.

questions.question: Which body is the final authority on disciplinary appeals under the Public Service Rules?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Civil Service Commission is the final authority on disciplinary appeals under the Public Service Rules.

questions.question: Within what maximum period should appeals against disciplinary decisions be concluded under the Public Service Rules?
questions.explanation: Appeals against disciplinary decisions under the Public Service Rules should be concluded within a maximum period of six months.

questions.question: Under the Public Service Rules, what disciplinary category covers falsification of official records or documents?
questions.explanation: Falsification of official records or documents is treated as Serious Misconduct under the Public Service Rules.

questions.question: What pay status applies to an officer on interdiction under the Public Service Rules?
questions.explanation: An officer placed on interdiction receives half salary pending the outcome of the investigation.

questions.question: What pay status applies to an officer placed on suspension pending investigation for gross misconduct?
questions.explanation: An officer on suspension pending investigation for gross misconduct does not receive salary during the suspension period.

questions.question: Which disciplinary penalty is expressly prohibited under the PSR, even for misconduct?
questions.explanation: The PSR expressly prohibits fines as a disciplinary penalty, even when misconduct is proved.

subcategories.name: Leave, Absence & Holidays
subcategories.description: Annual leave, special leave, absence, and holiday provisions.

questions.question: What is the minimum annual leave entitlement for officers on Grade Level 07 and above according to **PSR** 2021?
questions.explanation: PSR 120101 provides that officers on GL.07 and above are entitled to 28 working days annual leave in each leave year.

questions.question: How many working days of annual leave are officers on **GL.06** and below entitled to?
questions.explanation: PSR 120101 specifies that officers on GL.06 and below are entitled to 21 working days annual leave each year.

questions.question: What period counts as the leave year in the Federal Public Service?
questions.explanation: PSR 120102 states that the leave year begins on 1 January and ends on 31 December. The correct option therefore covers the January-to-December period.

questions.question: Which **PSR** provision governs leave entitlement for officers who have not completed 12 months of continuous service?
questions.explanation: PSR 120104 states that officers who have not completed 12 months continuous service are entitled to leave proportionate to service rendered.

questions.question: How many days of annual leave may be deferred to the next leave year with proper approval?
questions.explanation: PSR 120105 allows a maximum of 14 days to be deferred to the next leave year with appropriate approval.

questions.question: What is the minimum qualifying service before an officer can proceed on annual leave?
questions.explanation: PSR 120103 requires an officer to complete a minimum of 12 months of continuous service before becoming eligible for annual leave.

questions.question: Which authority may approve annual leave for officers on **GL.13** and above?
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: PSR 120107 states that annual leave for officers on GL.13 and above shall be approved by the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: Officers who cannot be spared during the leave year may be granted how many days leave in lieu with monetary compensation
questions.explanation: PSR 120106 allows officers who cannot be spared to take up to 14 days leave in lieu, with leave allowance paid in addition.

questions.question: How is annual leave calculated in **PSR** 2021?
options: On a monthly basis at 1/12th per month of service.
questions.explanation: PSR 120104 provides that annual leave accrues monthly at the rate of one-twelfth of the total annual entitlement.

questions.question: What is the leave allowance percentage payable on annual basic salary?
questions.explanation: PSR 120108 provides that leave allowance is 10% of annual basic salary, payable once a year to every officer proceeding on leave.

questions.question: What is the duration of maternity leave under **PSR** 2021?
questions.explanation: PSR 120201 states that female officers are entitled to 16 weeks maternity leave with full pay.

questions.question: When does maternity leave normally commence?
options: At least 4 weeks before the expected date of delivery.
questions.explanation: PSR 120201 provides that maternity leave begins 4 weeks before the expected date of delivery.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of maternity leaves a female officer can enjoy in her career?
questions.explanation: PSR 120201(b) limits maternity leave to a maximum of four times during an officer’s career.

questions.question: What is the duration of paternity leave approved under **PSR** 2021?
questions.explanation: PSR 120202 provides that male officers are entitled to 10 working days paternity leave after the birth of a child.

questions.question: How many days of casual leave may an officer be granted per year?
questions.explanation: PSR 120109 allows up to 10 working days of casual leave per year, not deductible from annual leave.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule provides for study leave with pay for officers undergoing approved courses relevant to their duties?
questions.explanation: PSR 120207 grants study leave with pay for approved courses relevant to the officer’s duties and beneficial to the Service.

questions.question: What is the maximum period for study leave with pay under **PSR**'?
questions.explanation: PSR 120207(b) provides that study leave with pay shall not exceed 36 months at a stretch.

questions.question: Who must approve study leave with pay exceeding one year?
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office with FCSC approval.
Federal Civil Service Commission acting alone without the MDA head.
questions.explanation: Study leave with pay exceeding one year requires approval through the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office and the relevant approval of the Federal Civil Service Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the consequence of failing to resume duty after the expiration of approved leave" according to official standards?
options: Loss of pay for the excess days with possible disciplinary action.
questions.explanation: Failure to resume duty after approved leave expires may lead to loss of pay for the excess days and disciplinary action under the PSR.

questions.question: How many months of sick leave with full pay are officers entitled to within any 12-month period?
questions.explanation: PSR 120203(a) provides six months sick leave with full pay within a 12-month period, extendable under medical advice.

questions.question: What authority must certify an officer’s illness before granting sick leave exceeding two days?
questions.explanation: PSR 120203(c) requires certification by a Government Medical Officer for sick leave beyond two working days.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule provides for pre-retirement leave?
questions.explanation: PSR 120211 grants pre-retirement leave of three months to officers retiring voluntarily or compulsorily.

questions.question: Public holidays in the Federal Service are declared by which authority
options: The President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
questions.explanation: PSR 120209 provides that public holidays are declared by the President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the officers who are hospitalized while on annual leave are entitled to?
options: Conversion of the affected period to sick leave.
questions.explanation: PSR 120203(e) provides that hospitalization during leave converts the affected period to sick leave upon certification.

questions.question: Under **PSR** 2021, how many times may an officer be granted study leave without pay
questions.explanation: PSR 120208 restricts study leave without pay to once during an officer’s career, unless otherwise approved by the Federal Civil Service Commission.

questions.question: How long must an officer serve after returning from study leave with pay before applying for another?
questions.explanation: PSR 120207(e) requires an officer to serve at least three years after resumption before being eligible for another study leave with pay.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer who fails to resume duty after study leave without valid reason shall be treated as?
options: To have resigned from the service.
questions.explanation: PSR 120208(b) provides that failure to resume after study leave without valid reason is deemed resignation from the Service.

questions.question: Which rule specifies the treatment of unauthorized absence from duty?
questions.explanation: PSR 100308 (linked to Chapter 10) states that unauthorized absence for 10 consecutive working days constitutes abandonment of duty.

questions.question: What is the maximum period for leave on compassionate grounds under **PSR**'?
questions.explanation: PSR 120205 provides that compassionate leave not exceeding 14 working days may be granted in deserving cases.

questions.question: Who approves sick leave exceeding 42 days?
options: Permanent Secretary on advice of a Government Medical Board.
questions.explanation: PSR 120203(d) requires approval of the Permanent Secretary based on recommendation from a Government Medical Board for sick leave exceeding 42 days.

questions.question: What happens when a public holiday falls within an officer's approved annual leave period?
options: Addition of the day to the leave period.
Counting of the day as part of the approved leave.
Return to duty on the holiday.
questions.explanation: A public holiday that falls within approved annual leave counts as part of the leave period rather than extending it.

questions.question: Officers on what category of leave are not entitled to leave allowance
questions.explanation: PSR 120207(f) stipulates that officers on study leave with pay are not entitled to leave allowance during the period.

questions.question: How many consecutive years may an officer defer annual leave before it is forfeited?
questions.explanation: PSR 120105 allows deferment of leave for only one subsequent year; thereafter, unused leave is forfeited.

questions.question: Under **PSR** 2021, officers on **GL.14** and above must obtain whose approval to travel abroad while on leave
options: Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: PSR 120107(c) requires officers on GL.14 and above to obtain clearance from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation before travelling abroad while on leave.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer on approved study leave with pay who withdraws from the course without permission shall?
options: Refund emoluments received during the leave.
questions.explanation: PSR 120207(g) requires refund of all emoluments by any officer who withdraws from an approved course without authorization.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the during pre-retirement leave, the officer is entitled to?
questions.explanation: PSR 120211(b) provides that pre-retirement leave is with full pay and benefits.

questions.question: What is the main difference between study leave with pay and without pay?
questions.explanation: PSR 120207 and 120208 distinguish between study leave with pay (for relevant approved courses) and without pay (for unrelated or self-sponsored study).

questions.question: Who approves compassionate leave beyond 14 days?
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: PSR 120205(b) provides that compassionate leave exceeding 14 days requires approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

questions.question: Which **PSR** provision deals with leave for officers posted to missions abroad?
questions.explanation: PSR 130206 governs leave for officers serving in Nigeria’s missions abroad, applied in conjunction with Chapter 12.

questions.question: What should be attached to leave applications according to **PSR**'?
options: Leave roster and duty relief arrangement.
questions.explanation: PSR 120107(d) requires officers to attach leave roster and duty relief arrangements to their leave applications.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer who over-stays approved leave without permission commits?
questions.explanation: PSR 120110 treats over-staying leave as serious misconduct punishable under disciplinary rules.

questions.question: Can a nursing mother combine maternity leave with annual leave
options: Yes, with approval of Permanent Secretary.
No, maternity leave must be taken separately.
questions.explanation: PSR 120201(c) allows maternity leave to be combined with annual leave upon approval by the Permanent Secretary.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if a public holiday is declared during pre-retirement leave, the officer?
options: Remains on leave as the day counts within it.
Returns to duty for the holiday.
questions.explanation: PSR 120209 clarifies that public holidays falling during any form of leave, including pre-retirement, count within the leave period.

questions.question: What is the consequence for an officer who overstays study leave without approval?
options: Deemed to have resigned from the Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 120208(c) provides that overstay beyond approved study leave period without approval is treated as resignation.

questions.question: Officers granted paternity leave must apply within what period after the birth of a child
questions.explanation: PSR 120202(b) provides that paternity leave applications must be made within 14 days of the child’s birth.

questions.question: Under **PSR**, what happens to leave entitlement when an officer dies in service
options: Operationally, leave lapses automatically under normal controls.
Monetary value of unused leave is paid to next of kin.
In practice, leave days are transferred in authorized administrative workflow.
Under formal rules, no action is taken under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: PSR 120210 provides that the monetary value of accrued leave of a deceased officer shall be paid to next of kin.

questions.question: How is leave entitlement affected when an officer is interdicted?
options: Suspension of leave entitlement until reinstatement.
questions.explanation: An officer under interdiction does not continue to enjoy normal leave entitlement until the matter is resolved and the officer is reinstated where appropriate.

questions.question: Who maintains the leave roster in each Ministry or Department?
options: Director of Administration or Human Resource Officer.
questions.explanation: PSR 120107(a) assigns the preparation and maintenance of leave rosters to the Director of Administration or HR officer.

questions.question: Under **PSR** 2021, leave rosters should be prepared and approved no later than which month each year
questions.explanation: PSR 120107(b) stipulates that annual leave rosters should be prepared and approved by January each year to ensure orderly scheduling.

questions.question: In the context of Leave, Absence & Holidays, which action best demonstrates sound governance?
options: Use of approved leave procedures with complete records.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Sound governance in leave administration depends on approved procedures, complete records, and decisions that can be reviewed later.

questions.question: For effective leave, absence & holidays, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure leave, absence & holidays compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on escalation of material compliance gaps within Leave, Absence & Holidays?
options: Early identification of control gaps with prompt escalation.
questions.explanation: Escalation of material compliance gaps is strongest when control weaknesses are identified early and referred promptly for higher review.

questions.question: When handling leave, absence & holidays, which choice reflects proper appointment governance standards?
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain disciplinary process in Leave, Absence & Holidays?
options: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Leave, Absence & Holidays, which action best demonstrates leave administration
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective Leave, Absence & Holidays administration, which approach best preserves promotion standards?
options: Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards are protected when eligibility is confirmed before any advancement recommendation is made.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on circular compliance within Leave, Absence & Holidays?
options: Align practice with current circular directives and PSR rules.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Align practice with current circular directives and psr rules. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays.

questions.question: When handling Leave, Absence & Holidays matters, which choice best reflects proper documented procedure?
options: Use of documented procedure with complete recordkeeping.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Proper documented procedure means following the approved steps and keeping complete records that support later review.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Leave, Absence & Holidays?
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Leave, Absence & Holidays, which action best demonstrates public accountability?
questions.explanation: Public accountability is strongest when decisions are traceable and supported by evidence-based reasons.

questions.question: For effective leave, absence & holidays, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on risk control within Leave, Absence & Holidays?
options: Early risk identification with documented mitigation.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Risk control improves when risks are identified early, mitigation is documented, and corrective action is tracked.

questions.question: When handling leave, absence & holidays, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards?
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain operational discipline in Leave, Absence & Holidays?
options: Approved workflow use with output verification.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Operational discipline is sustained when approved workflows are followed and outputs are checked before closure.

questions.question: In the context of Leave, Absence & Holidays, which action best demonstrates citizen-focused service
options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective Leave, Absence & Holidays administration, which approach best preserves record management?
options: Accurate file maintenance with status updates.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Record management is preserved by accurate file maintenance and timely status updates at each control point.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on performance standards within Leave, Absence & Holidays?
options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: When handling Leave, Absence & Holidays matters, which choice best reflects sound governance standards?
options: Use of approved leave procedures with complete records.
questions.explanation: Sound governance standards require approved procedures, complete records, and decisions that can be reviewed objectively.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain leave, absence & holidays compliance in Leave, Absence & Holidays?
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Leave, Absence & Holidays, which action best demonstrates leave, absence & holidays risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective leave, absence & holidays, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure appointment governance
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on disciplinary process within Leave, Absence & Holidays?
options: Due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: A sound disciplinary process depends on due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions that can withstand review.

questions.question: When handling leave, absence & holidays, which choice reflects proper leave administration standards?
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in leave, absence & holidays Reference: Leave, Absence & Holidays - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain promotion standards in Leave, Absence & Holidays administration?
options: Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards remain credible when eligibility is confirmed before advancement is recommended.

questions.question: A unit handling Leave, Absence & Holidays receives a case with competing priorities. Which action best preserves compliance and service quality?
options: Bypassed review checkpoints under time pressure.
Consistent application of PSR provisions with auditable records.
questions.explanation: Compliance and service quality are preserved when the applicable PSR provisions are applied consistently and the record remains auditable.

questions.question: What procedure applies when an officer wishes to resign from the service?
options: They must apply for a leave of absence.
They must submit their resignation letter to the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must give one month's notice of their intention to resign.
They must give three months' notice.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They must give one month's notice of their intention to resign.). Rule 020904 states that an officer who wishes to resign must give "a month's notice."

questions.question: Select the statement that correctly defines the condition regarding transfers or lateral conversions into the Foreign Service?
options: It is not allowed above GL 10 due to the uniqueness of the Foreign Service.
It is a common practice for promotions.
It is encouraged for experienced staff from other ministries.
It is permissible with special approval from the President.3.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (It is not allowed above GL 10 due to the uniqueness of the Foreign Service.). Due to the unique nature of the Foreign Service, lateral conversions or transfers into it are not permitted for officers above Grade Level

questions.question: Which condition applies before a non-pensionable officer may be granted leave?
options: Leave at the sole discretion of the Permanent Secretary.
Entitlement to leave in accordance with Chapter Twelve of the Rules.
Automatic annual leave without reference to the Rules.
No leave entitlement under the Rules.
questions.explanation: Rule 021214 provides that a non-pensionable officer may be granted leave only in accordance with the provisions of Chapter Twelve of the Public Service Rules.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the role of the completed personal emolument form under FR" according to official standards?
options: Basis for opening audited group registers before payroll inclusion.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1521(ii) states that the completed personal emolument form is used to open the group registers, which must be audited before an officer is placed on payroll.

questions.question: What consequence follows if a Foreign Service officer's marriage to a foreigner is judged not to be in the interest of the Service?
options: Exit from the Foreign Service or return to the Home Service.
Automatic demotion to a lower grade.
questions.explanation: If a Foreign Service officer's marriage to a foreigner is judged not to be in the interest of the Service, the officer must leave the Foreign Service or return to the Home Service.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the consequence of reproducing any part of the 2021 edition of the Public Service Rules without express approval or authorization" according to official standards?
options: Exposure to a minor fine only.
Prohibition on reproduction without express approval because of copyright protection.
questions.explanation: The 2021 edition of the Public Service Rules is copyrighted, so reproduction of any part requires express approval or authorization from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the minimum age for eligibility for appointment into the Federal Public Service" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: Rule 020206(a) provides that an applicant must be not less than 18 years and not more than 50 years of age to be eligible for appointment. The minimum age being tested is therefore 18 years.

questions.question: An officer wishing to transfer from one senior post to another within the Federal Public Service must have served for a minimum period of how many months?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (6 months). Rule 020307 states that an officer must have served for a minimum period of six months in his original post before seeking a transfer to another post.

questions.question: The consequence for an officer whose office is no longer demand due to the abolition of office?
options: They will be redeployed to another office.
They will be offered a new appointment.
They will be compulsorily retired from the Service.
They will be placed on a leave of absence. □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They will be compulsorily retired from the Service.). Rule 020907 states that an officer whose service is no longer necessitate due to the abolition of office "must be necessitate to leave the Service." Part 12: Mandatory Contribution to Pension Scheme

questions.question: What happens if a non-pensionable officer is compelled to resign due to ill health not caused by their own negligence?
options: The officer will receive a full month's salary.
The officer will not receive any salary.
The officer will receive a severance allowance. a.
The officer must receive such salary as may be due up to the date of such resignation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The officer must receive such salary as may be due up to the date of such resignation.). Rule 021207 states that if an officer is compelled to resign due to ill health, "he must payment such salary as may be due up to the date of such resignation or the date on which a certificate in that respect was issued.

questions.question: The consequence for an officer who fails to pass the compulsory confirmation examination within the stipulated period?
options: The officer is suspended without payment. □.
The officer is demoted to a lower grade level.
The officer is granted an extension to retake the exam.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The officer's appointment is terminated.). Rule 030104 clearly states that failure to pass the examination within the specified time must result in the termination of the officer's appointment.

questions.question: The condition for using the COMPRO result for promotion from a junior to a senior post?
options: The officer must be recommended by the Permanent Secretary. □.
The officer must have served for a minimum of two years.
The officer must have passed the exam on their first attempt.
The officer must meet other eligibility criteria for the senior post.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The officer must meet other eligibility criteria for the senior post.). Rule 030502 outlines that the result is used as a prerequisite, but the officer must still meet other conditions for promotion.

questions.question: When should a refund of a deposit be made?
options: Refund at the end of the financial year.
Refund as soon as due after completion of the necessary checks.
Refund only with explicit approval of the Minister of Finance.
Refund only when the deposit account has a sufficient balance.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1307 states that refunds of deposits are made as soon as they are due, provided the necessary checks and reconciliations have been completed.

questions.question: Which role has the constitutional power to appoint persons to offices in the Federal Civil Service and exercise disciplinary control over them?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).). The FCSC is the constitutional body that has the power to appoint persons to offices in the Federal Civil Service and exercise disciplinary control over them.

questions.question: An officer can only claim to be acting in a superior post if which condition is met?
options: They have served in the post for more than 6 months.
Their Permanent Secretary gives a verbal approval. □.
They have been asked to cover the schedule of the superior officer.
The acting appointment has been expressly approved in writing by the constituted authority.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The acting appointment has been expressly approved in writing by the constituted authority.). For an officer to be able to claim to be acting on a particular post, such an arrangement must be expressly approved in writing by the constituted authority.

questions.question: The 2021 Edition of the Public Service Rules is a review of which previous edition?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (2008 Edition). The foreword mentions, "This edition of the Public Service Rules is therefore a review of the 2008 edition."

questions.question: The incremental date for an officer appointed or promoted to a post in the Federal Public Service?
options: The date of assumption of duty.
The first day of the month of appointment.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (1st January of every year). Rule 020104 establishes that the incremental date of an officer is the 1st of January every year, provided the officer has spent at least six months on the grade with good performance before enjoying the increment.

questions.question: What must a person previously employed in the public service, and dismissed or called upon to resign, obtain before reappointment?
options: Approval of the Commission irrespective of delegated powers.
Prior approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation only.
questions.explanation: Rule 020207(i)(b) requires such a person to obtain the approval of the Commission, irrespective of any delegation of the Commission's powers, before reappointment can be considered.

questions.question: What document is demand for appointment to senior posts?
options: Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 1).
Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 2).
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 2)). Rule 020208(a) specifies that all applicants for senior posts must submit a completed "Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 2)".

questions.question: Choose the best answer for: What form should an officer newly appointed to a pensionable establishment use to provide their personal particulars?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Gen 60). Rule 020209 states that a newly appointed officer is demand to enter his/her personal particulars on a Staff document Form (Gen 60).

questions.question: Which option aligns with the meaning of the requirement for Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices regarding transfers on junior posts?
options: They must obtain approval from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must not accept any staff seconded without the endorsement of the Federal Civil Service Commission/Board.
They must conduct an interview for the staff a.
They must notify the Federal Civil Service Commission immediately.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (They must not accept any staff seconded without the endorsement of the Federal Civil Service Commission/Board.). Rule 020507(j) states that "No Ministry must accept any staff seconded to it without the endorsement of the Federal Civil Service Commission/Board."

questions.question: The requirement for MDAs regarding staff on transfer/secondment?
options: They must seek approval from the Federal Civil Service Commission for every transfer.
They must submit a report every six months.
They must make quarterly returns to the Federal Civil Service Commission and the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must sustain a list of all seconded staff.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They must make quarterly returns to the Federal Civil Service Commission and the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.). Rule 020509 states that MDAs "must make quarterly returns to the Federal Civil 27 Service Commission and the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation on the number of staff on transfer/secondment." Part 8: Posting of Officers

questions.question: When can an acting appointment be made?
options: Appointment upon mere consideration for promotion.
Appointment where a duty post of not less than SGL 14 remains unattended because of the substantive holder's absence.
Appointment only when a substantive post becomes permanently vacant.
Appointment only for a very short period without reference to post level.
questions.explanation: Rule 020701 provides that an acting appointment may be made where a duty post not lower than SGL 14 is unattended because the substantive holder is on leave, secondment, or otherwise unavailable.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the effective date of an acting appointment" according to official standards?
options: Date of notification to the Federal Civil Service Commission.
Date of the gazette notice alone.
Date the officer substantively assumes the duties of the post.
questions.explanation: Rule 020705 provides that the acting appointment takes effect from the date the officer substantively takes over the duties and responsibilities of the post, as authorized in the gazette notice.

questions.question: Which option best describes the accountability of ministries regarding acting appointments?
options: They must conduct a fresh promotion exercise.
They must obtain approval from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must give a written test to the officer. □.
They must inform the Federal Civil Service Commission about the acting appointment.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They must inform the Federal Civil Service Commission about the acting appointment.). Rule 020707 specifies that ministries "must be necessitate to notify the Federal Civil Service Commission when officers revert from acting appointments."

questions.question: What is the penalty for an officer who accepts a paid appointment during leave of absence without prior approval?
options: The officer shall not accept paid employment without the express permission of the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
Procedurally, warning, within established administrative procedures under applicable policy conditions.
Payment of a fine, in line with formal public service practice.
Within formal administration, withheld increment, subject to existing regulatory standards under due process conditions.
questions.explanation: An Officer on leave of absence shall not accept any paid employment without previously obtaining the express approval of the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).

subcategories.name: Allowances, Pay & Benefits
subcategories.description: Allowances, pay administration, and benefits under PSR.

questions.question: What **PSR** provision governs payment of duty tour allowance?
questions.explanation: PSR 130101 defines duty tour allowance as payment to cover expenses incurred by an officer on official assignment away from duty station.

questions.question: Which officer authorizes duty tour for a staff within the same station?
questions.explanation: PSR 130102(a) empowers the Head of Department to approve duty tours within the same station.

questions.question: What is the official term for payment covering overnight accommodation while on duty outside headquarters?
questions.explanation: PSR 130104 states that Estacode is payable to officers on duty outside Nigeria or for overnight official assignments.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the responsibility allowance is payable to officers who?
options: Hold additional official duties beyond their schedule.
questions.explanation: PSR 130106 provides that responsibility allowance compensates officers who perform additional official responsibilities beyond their normal schedule.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule regulates acting allowance for officers performing duties of a higher post?
questions.explanation: PSR 130107 defines acting allowance as compensation for officers temporarily performing functions of a higher position.

questions.question: An officer must act in a higher post for a minimum of how many days before qualifying for acting allowance
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(b) specifies that acting allowance is payable only if the officer acts for not less than 21 consecutive days.

questions.question: Who approves acting appointments in the Federal Civil Service?
questions.explanation: PSR 020707 and 130107(c) state that the Federal Civil Service Commission approves acting appointments in the Federal Civil Service.

questions.question: What is the purpose of hardship allowance as provided in **PSR** 2021?
options: For officers in difficult terrains or conditions of service.
questions.explanation: PSR 130109 establishes hardship allowance for officers serving in locations with difficult terrain or adverse conditions.

questions.question: Transport allowance is intended to cover what
questions.explanation: PSR 130110 provides that transport allowance is granted to officers to cover cost of transportation from home to office and back.

questions.question: Who determines the rate of overtime allowance in the public service?
options: National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130111 stipulates that the National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission determines overtime allowance rates for public servants.

questions.question: What **PSR** provision governs meal subsidy allowance?
questions.explanation: PSR 130112 provides that meal subsidy allowance is payable to officers who are not provided with meals while on duty under certain conditions.

questions.question: Which allowance is payable to compensate officers working under hazardous conditions?
questions.explanation: PSR 130113 states that hazard allowance is granted to officers working under dangerous or unhealthy conditions.

questions.question: What allowance may be paid to officers who use their private vehicles for official duty?
questions.explanation: PSR 130114 allows payment of mileage allowance to officers who use private vehicles for official assignments with prior approval.

questions.question: When does acting allowance cease to be payable?
options: Cessation when the officer resumes the substantive post.
Automatic cessation after one year of acting service.
Cessation only upon substantive promotion approval.
questions.explanation: Acting allowance ceases when the officer resumes the substantive post or otherwise stops performing the duties that justified the acting appointment.

questions.question: Which authority determines local duty tour allowance rates within Nigeria?
options: National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: PSR 130101(b) provides that the National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission determines local duty tour rates.

questions.question: Who approves overtime work schedules in **MDAs**'?
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: PSR 130111(b) requires that overtime work schedules be approved by the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office before implementation.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule governs payment of acting allowance to an officer on leave?
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(e) provides that an officer on leave is not entitled to acting allowance during the period of absence.

questions.question: Which of the following allowances is taxable under the **PSR**'?
questions.explanation: PSR 130106(c) provides that responsibility allowance is taxable as part of emoluments.

questions.question: An officer entitled to overtime pay cannot work more than how many hours of overtime per day
questions.explanation: PSR 130111(d) limits overtime to a maximum of 4 hours per day except in emergencies.

questions.question: Which allowance is paid to compensate officers for performing extra duties on weekends or public holidays?
questions.explanation: PSR 130111 provides overtime allowance for extra work beyond normal hours, including weekends and holidays.

questions.question: Who authorizes the payment of duty tour allowance outside Nigeria?
options: President through the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130104 assigns authorization for foreign duty tour allowance to the President through OHCSF.

questions.question: What is the definition of salary according to **PSR** 2021?
options: All cash payments received by an officer in a month.
Basic pay and all taxable allowances.
questions.explanation: PSR 140101 defines salary as the basic pay plus all taxable allowances due to an officer monthly.

questions.question: Salaries of officers are paid how frequently according to **PSR**'
questions.explanation: PSR 140102 stipulates that salaries are paid monthly in arrears on or before the last working day of each month.

questions.question: Under **PSR**, who is responsible for certifying payment vouchers for salary
questions.explanation: PSR 140104 assigns responsibility for certifying salary payment vouchers to the Director of Finance and Accounts.

questions.question: What **PSR** rule authorizes the granting of salary advances to officers?
questions.explanation: PSR 140201 authorizes the granting of salary advances to officers under conditions specified by the Financial Regulations.

questions.question: Salary advances granted to officers must not exceed how many months of basic salary
questions.explanation: PSR 140201(b) limits salary advances to one month’s basic salary, recoverable within three months.

questions.question: Who approves the grant of salary advance in Ministries and Departments?
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: PSR 140201(c) provides that salary advances are approved by the Accounting Officer—Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: What **PSR** rule governs payment of transport allowance during transfer of duty station?
questions.explanation: PSR 130115 authorizes transport allowance for officers officially transferred to new duty stations.

questions.question: Who determines the rates for Estacode and duty tour allowances abroad?
options: Office of the Head of Civil Service.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission with Presidential approval.
questions.explanation: PSR 130104(b) provides that NSIWC determines rates for foreign Estacode subject to Presidential approval.

questions.question: When is an officer entitled to passage allowance?
questions.explanation: Passage allowance is payable when an officer is posted on transfer in circumstances that involve relocation as provided by the applicable rules.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the meaning of 'allowance' under the PSR 2021" according to official standards?
options: General extra payment for every officer.
Financial benefit meant for a specific official expenditure or condition of service.
Compulsory monthly benefit payable in every workflow.
questions.explanation: Under the PSR, an allowance is a financial benefit attached to a specific official expenditure, responsibility, or condition of service rather than a general bonus.

questions.question: What is the objective of responsibility allowance?
options: To compensate for additional administrative burdens.
questions.explanation: PSR 130106(a) states that responsibility allowance compensates officers with administrative or supervisory burdens beyond normal schedule.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule provides for hardship allowance?
questions.explanation: PSR 130109 provides for hardship allowance payable to officers serving in difficult terrain or adverse environmental conditions.

questions.question: Under **PSR**, what is the difference between hazard and hardship allowances
options: Hazard is for dangerous conditions; hardship is for difficult living conditions.
Hazard is payable monthly, hardship annually.
Hardship is taxable, hazard is not.
questions.explanation: PSR 130109–130113 differentiate hardship (for adverse terrain) and hazard (for dangerous work environments).

questions.question: Overtime allowance shall not be paid to officers on which grade levels
questions.explanation: PSR 130111(c) states that officers on GL.07 and above are not entitled to overtime allowance.

questions.question: Which allowance is payable to cover minor operational expenses during official travel?
questions.explanation: PSR 130101 describes duty tour allowance as payment for incidental and operational expenses during official assignments.

questions.question: Payment of any allowance not recognized by **PSR** requires approval from which body
options: Office of the Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130100(c) requires NSIWC approval before introducing or varying any allowance not already recognized in PSR.

questions.question: How long may an officer act in a higher position before confirmation or replacement is required?
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(f) limits acting appointments to six months before confirmation or substitution.

questions.question: What **PSR** rule regulates loan advances to officers?
questions.explanation: PSR 140205 allows government to grant loan advances to officers for specific approved purposes, subject to Financial Regulations.

questions.question: Salary may be stopped if an officer is absent from duty without leave for how long
questions.explanation: PSR 100308 provides that salary is stopped after 10 consecutive working days of absence without leave.

questions.question: When does responsibility allowance cease?
options: Cessation upon any transfer of the officer.
Cessation whenever the officer proceeds on leave.
Cessation when the officer no longer holds the additional responsibility.
questions.explanation: Responsibility allowance ceases once the officer no longer performs the additional responsibility for which the allowance was approved.

questions.question: What authority approves the conversion of unclaimed overtime to time off?
options: National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130111(e) provides that unclaimed overtime may be converted to time off only with approval of the Permanent Secretary.

questions.question: Who authorizes the payment of hazard allowance in Ministries?
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130113(b) vests authority to pay hazard allowance in the Accounting Officer of the Ministry or Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 2021, salary arrears may be paid for a period not exceeding?
questions.explanation: PSR 140103(b) restricts payment of salary arrears to a period not exceeding twelve months, except with special approval.

questions.question: Which **PSR** provision deals with payment of retirement benefits?
questions.explanation: PSR 090202 in Chapter 9 governs payment of pensions and gratuities as retirement benefits to eligible officers.

questions.question: Who certifies that an officer has not been overpaid before final salary payment on exit?
questions.explanation: PSR 140204 assigns the Director of Finance and Accounts responsibility for certifying that officers are not overpaid before final payment.

questions.question: What **PSR** rule governs recovery of overpayment from an officer’s salary?
questions.explanation: PSR 140207 provides that any overpayment to an officer shall be recovered from salary or other entitlements in line with Financial Regulations.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr**, no allowance shall be paid to an officer on interdiction except?
questions.explanation: PSR 100305(d) states that no allowances are payable to officers on interdiction until reinstatement.

questions.question: Salary payment to officers should not be made in advance of work except when what condition applies?
options: Advance payment whenever an officer proceeds on leave.
Advance payment whenever an officer retires from service.
Advance payment where the Accounting Officer has specifically approved it.
Advance payment whenever an officer makes a personal request.
questions.explanation: Salary should not be paid in advance except where the Accounting Officer has specifically approved the advance under the applicable financial rules.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 2021, what authority reviews and updates allowances across the Public Service
options: Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130100(b) empowers the National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC) to review, approve, and update all public service allowances periodically.

questions.question: In the context of Allowances, Pay & Benefits, which action best demonstrates sound governance?
options: Use of approved procedures with complete records.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Sound governance in allowances, pay, and benefits depends on approved procedures, complete records, and decisions that can be reviewed later.

questions.question: For effective allowances, pay & benefits, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure allowances, pay & benefits compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on escalation of material compliance gaps within Allowances, Pay & Benefits?
options: Early identification of control gaps with prompt escalation.
questions.explanation: Escalation of material compliance gaps is strongest when control weaknesses are identified early and referred promptly for higher review.

questions.question: When handling allowances, pay & benefits, which choice reflects proper appointment governance standards?
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain disciplinary process in Allowances, Pay & Benefits?
options: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Allowances, Pay & Benefits, which action best demonstrates leave administration
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective Allowances, Pay & Benefits administration, which approach best preserves promotion standards?
options: Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards are protected when eligibility is confirmed before any advancement recommendation is made.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on circular compliance within Allowances, Pay & Benefits?
options: Align practice with current circular directives and PSR rules.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Align practice with current circular directives and psr rules. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits.

questions.question: When handling Allowances, Pay & Benefits matters, which choice best reflects proper documented procedure?
options: Use of documented procedure with complete recordkeeping.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Proper documented procedure means following the approved steps and keeping complete records that support later review.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Allowances, Pay & Benefits?
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Allowances, Pay & Benefits, which action best demonstrates public accountability?
questions.explanation: Public accountability is strongest when decisions are traceable and supported by evidence-based reasons.

questions.question: For effective allowances, pay & benefits, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on risk control within Allowances, Pay & Benefits?
options: Early risk identification with documented mitigation.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Risk control improves when risks are identified early, mitigation is documented, and corrective action is tracked.

questions.question: When handling allowances, pay & benefits, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards?
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain operational discipline in Allowances, Pay & Benefits?
options: Approved workflow use with output verification.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Operational discipline is sustained when approved workflows are followed and outputs are checked before closure.

questions.question: In the context of Allowances, Pay & Benefits, which action best demonstrates citizen-focused service
options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective Allowances, Pay & Benefits administration, which approach best preserves record management?
options: Accurate file maintenance with status updates.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Record management is preserved by accurate file maintenance and timely status updates at each control point.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on performance standards within Allowances, Pay & Benefits?
options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: When handling Allowances, Pay & Benefits matters, which choice best reflects sound governance standards?
options: Use of approved procedures with complete records.
questions.explanation: Sound governance standards require approved procedures, complete records, and decisions that can be reviewed objectively.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain allowances, pay & benefits compliance in Allowances, Pay & Benefits?
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Allowances, Pay & Benefits, which action best demonstrates allowances, pay & benefits risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective allowances, pay & benefits, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure appointment governance
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on disciplinary process within Allowances, Pay & Benefits?
options: Due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: A sound disciplinary process depends on due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions that can withstand review.

questions.question: When handling allowances, pay & benefits, which choice reflects proper leave administration standards?
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain promotion standards in Allowances, Pay & Benefits administration?
options: Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards remain credible when eligibility is confirmed before advancement is recommended.

questions.question: What are "Junior Posts"?
options: Posts in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices attracting emoluments on Grade Level 06 and below and their equivalent.
questions.explanation: Junior Posts are posts in Ministries and Extra-Ministerial Offices that attract emoluments on Grade Level 06 and below, including their equivalents.

questions.question: What are "Senior Posts"?
options: Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level GL.07 and above and other posts attracting fixed emoluments of equivalent Grade Levels in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level GL.07 and above and other posts attracting fixed emoluments of equivalent Grade Levels in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices.).

questions.question: Which section of the Code of Conduct addresses the issue of receiving benefits?
questions.explanation: Section 11 of the Code of Conduct addresses receiving benefits, so that is the relevant provision.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of forwarding death-in-service documents according to the established standards?
options: Facilitate processing of benefits due to legal beneficiaries.
questions.explanation: Those documents are forwarded to process benefits due to the legal beneficiaries of the deceased officer.

questions.question: How is salary for part of a month calculated on retirement or termination of appointment?
options: Without payment for the incomplete month.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1504 provides that salary for part of a month on retirement or termination is calculated on a pro-rata basis.

questions.question: Select the statement that true about acting appointments?
options: They are made only when an officer is due for promotion.
They are not to be made unless absolutely necessary.
They are made for all grade levels. Q.
They are only for a duration of six months.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (They are not to be made unless absolutely necessary.). Rule 020702 states that an acting appointment should "not be made unless it is necessary." Part 10: Promotion

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "a key duty of the officer controlling expenditure under FR 1519" according to official standards?
options: Manual calculation of every salary item.
Personal approval of every salary payment.
Ensuring that standard personal emolument records show rate of pay, incremental date, allowances, and deductions.
Keeping every record personally under lock without payroll processing.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1519 requires the officer controlling expenditure to ensure that the standard personal emolument records accurately show the officer's rate of pay, incremental date, allowances, and deductions.

questions.question: An acting appointment is NOT a form of trial for what role?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Promotion). Rule 020703 explicitly states, "Acting appointment is not intended as a means of testing the suitability of officers for promotion."

questions.question: In which circumstance is an acting appointment not regarded as having ceased?
options: Casual or special leave taken by the acting officer.
Resumption of duty by the substantive holder.
Movement of the acting officer to another post.
Substantive promotion of the acting officer.
questions.explanation: Rule 020708 states that an acting officer is not regarded as relinquishing the acting appointment merely because the officer proceeds on casual or special leave.

questions.question: Responsible for forwarding departmental files and other documents necessary for processing retirement to PENCOM?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Departmental Pension Officer □). Rule 021003 states that "Forwarding of Departmental files and other documents not expressly mentioned in Rule 021002 must no longer be a condition for processing of retirement benefits." This implies that the Departmental Pension Officer is accountable for the overall processing. The previous rule, 021002, mentions that Departmental Pension Officers are demand to forward up-to-date records. Part 13: Certificate of Service

questions.question: For what promotion purpose may an officer's compulsory confirmation examination result be used?
options: Promotion to the next grade level in every case.
Promotion from a junior post to a senior post.
questions.explanation: Rule 030502 states that the compulsory confirmation examination result may be used for promotion from a junior post to a senior post, subject to other conditions.

questions.question: The fees paid to invigilators and examiners are typically separate from what other fees?
options: The invigilators' and examiners' regular salaries. □.
The fees paid by the candidates for the examination.
The transport allowances of the candidates.
The fees for setting the questions.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The invigilators' and examiners' regular salaries. □). Examination fees are a form of extra-duty allowance and are separate from an officer's regular salary

questions.question: Which payment is not exempt from Stamp Duty under all conditions?
options: Payments for goods supplied or services rendered at N1,500.00.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 620(b)(i) exempts payments for goods supplied or services rendered only when the amount is below the stated threshold, so a payment of N1,500.00 is not exempt under all conditions.

questions.question: Choose the best answer for: What does Financial Regulation 1401 primarily address?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1401 deals with personal advances, so that is the correct subject.

questions.question: According to Financial Regulation 1501, what is the definition of "Salary"?
options: The personal emolument paid to an employee, usually monthly, for services rendered at a predetermined rate.
An allowance for housing and transportation.
A one-time disbursement for a special project.
A bonus paid for good performance.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The personal emolument paid to an employee, usually monthly, for services rendered at a predetermined rate.). Financial Regulation 1501 defines salary as the personal emolument paid to an employee, usually monthly, for services rendered at a predetermined rate of disbursement

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the maximum duration of an acting appointment under Rule" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: Rule 020705 provides that an acting appointment must not exceed one year, although in exceptional circumstances it may be extended for another year. The maximum period stated in the rule is therefore one year in the first instance.

questions.question: What happens if an officer on an acting appointment proceeds on casual or special leave?
options: Continuation of the acting appointment without relinquishment of duties.
Automatic extension of the acting appointment.
questions.explanation: Rule 020708 states that an officer on an acting appointment who proceeds on casual or special leave is not regarded as relinquishing the duties and responsibilities of the acting appointment.

questions.question: The main aim of Rule?
options: To access contributions to the National Housing Fund.
To provide a schedule for non-pensionable appointments.
To workflow the retirement of an officer.
To secure officers receive their retirement benefits promptly.
