questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To secure officers receive their retirement benefits promptly.). Rule 021002 states that "In order to guarantee that officers receive their retirement benefits promptly, Departmental Pension Officers are demand to forward to the Pension Commission (PENCOM) an up-to-date log of service."

questions.question: An officer on a non-pensionable appointment must not directly or indirectly receive what?
options: Commission or profits of any kind, but will devote the whole of their time to the service of the Government.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Commission or profits of any kind, but will devote the whole of their time to the service of the Government.). Rule 021204 states that the person engaged must not "either directly or indirectly be engaged in or concerned with any other service or business whatsoever or receive commission or profits of any kind.

questions.question: The chief objective of the Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examination for Junior Officers?
options: To confirm their appointment and/or promote them.
To prepare them for promotion to senior posts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To confirm their appointment and/or promote them). The title of the section itself, "Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examinations," indicates its dual purpose20.

questions.question: An officer on probation who fails to pass the Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examination for Junior Officers is liable to what consequence?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Termination of their appointment). Rule 030104, which is a general rule for confirmation examinations, applies to all officers, including junior officers, stating that failure to pass leads to termination of appointment

questions.question: Which office must approve any variation in the Establishment or rates of allowances approve for the Public Service?
options: The Minister of Finance and the Office of the Head of Civil Service.
Only the Office of the Head of Civil Service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Minister of Finance and the Office of the Head of Civil Service.). Financial Regulation 311 states: "Any variation in the Establishment or rates of allowances... must receive the approval of the Minister of Finance and the Office of the Head of Civil Service..."

questions.question: What type of advance is referred to in Financial Regulation?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1405 specifically refers to salary advance on first appointment.

questions.question: How are salaries and allowances based on an annual rate paid?
options: Monthly installments of one twelfth of the annual rate.
As a lump sum at the end of the year. Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Monthly installments of one twelfth of the annual rate.). Financial Regulation 1504 specifies that salaries and allowances based on an annual rate must be paid by monthly installments of one twelfth of the annual rate.

subcategories.name: Conduct & Ethics
subcategories.description: Professional conduct and ethical obligations in service.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 2021, officers must maintain absolute loyalty to?
questions.explanation: PSR 040101 emphasizes that officers must demonstrate absolute loyalty to the Federal Republic of Nigeria and uphold the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the Code of Conduct for Public Officers under the **PSR**'?
options: To ensure accountability, transparency, and integrity in public service.
questions.explanation: Appendix I to PSR sets the Code of Conduct to ensure public officers act with integrity, transparency, and accountability.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the officers must not disclose official information without authorization as stated in?
questions.explanation: PSR 040203 prohibits disclosure of official information without due authorization, even after leaving service.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule prohibits public officers from engaging in political activities?
questions.explanation: PSR 040208 restricts public officers from active political activities, to maintain neutrality and objectivity.

questions.question: What is considered a breach of **PSR** 040205 regarding conflict of interest?
options: Accepting a contract in which the officer has personal interest.
questions.explanation: PSR 040205 forbids officers from engaging in any business or transaction where personal interest conflicts with official duty.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 040206, officers must not accept gifts or benefits except?
options: From personal friends on social occasions.
questions.explanation: PSR 040206 permits acceptance of gifts only from personal friends or relatives on non-official, social occasions.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 040209, public officers must not borrow money from?
questions.explanation: PSR 040209 forbids officers from borrowing from professional money lenders to avoid indebtedness and corruption risk.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 040207 prohibits officers from using their official positions to?
questions.explanation: PSR 040207 prohibits officers from using official positions to secure undue advantage for themselves, friends, or relations.

questions.question: Who has authority to investigate breaches of the Code of Conduct?
questions.explanation: Appendix I to PSR and the 5th Schedule of the Constitution assign the Code of Conduct Bureau to investigate such breaches.
questions.chapter: Appendix I – Code of Conduct Bureau

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 040204, officers must avoid?
options: Discrimination based on tribe, religion or gender.
questions.explanation: PSR 040204 mandates impartiality and forbids discrimination or favouritism based on religion, tribe, or gender.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the officers must declare their assets as required by?
options: Appendix I and the 5th Schedule of the Constitution.
questions.explanation: Appendix I requires officers to declare assets as mandated by the 5th Schedule of the 1999 Constitution Reference: Appendix I – Asset Declaration.

questions.question: What should an officer do if a potential conflict of interest arises?
options: Report it immediately to the Permanent Secretary.
Seek to benefit from it quietly.
Ask a colleague to act in his place.
questions.explanation: PSR 040205(b) requires officers to declare any potential conflict of interest to their superior for guidance.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 040210 provides that officers must avoid?
options: Indebtedness which may embarrass the Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 040210 directs officers to avoid excessive indebtedness that may compromise integrity or embarrass the Service.

questions.question: Who is responsible for enforcing discipline related to ethical breaches in Ministries?
questions.explanation: PSR 040103 assigns the Permanent Secretary the responsibility for enforcing discipline and ethical compliance in the Ministry.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 040201, officers must demonstrate?
options: Commitment to work ethics, diligence, and integrity.
questions.explanation: PSR 040201 directs all officers to maintain diligence, integrity, and discipline in the discharge of duties.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 040211, officers who fail to comply with the code of conduct may face?
questions.explanation: PSR 040211 and Appendix I specify that non-compliance with the Code of Conduct may lead to dismissal or other disciplinary sanctions.

questions.question: Under Appendix I, from whom must a public officer not accept gifts?
options: Persons who have official dealings with the officer.
Personal friends in private life only.
Officers working in the same ministry.
questions.explanation: Appendix I prohibits a public officer from accepting gifts or benefits from persons or bodies that have official dealings with the officer.

questions.question: Which ethical principle requires officers to avoid using government property for private gain?
questions.explanation: Appendix I emphasizes integrity, requiring officers to avoid using government resources for private benefit Reference: Appendix I – Integrity.

questions.question: What does **PSR** 040202 require regarding official correspondence?
options: Must be approved by the Permanent Secretary.
Handled in accordance with laid-down procedures and confidentiality.
questions.explanation: PSR 040202 mandates that official correspondence be handled confidentially and in accordance with approved procedures.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule requires public officers to treat members of the public with courtesy?
questions.explanation: PSR 040104 mandates officers to show courtesy and respect in dealing with the public.

questions.question: What should officers do when offered gifts that may appear as bribes?
options: Reject and report the offer immediately.
questions.explanation: PSR 040206(b) and Appendix I require officers to reject and report such offers to the appropriate authority.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under appendix i, officers must observe political neutrality and not?
options: Join political parties or attend partisan rallies.
questions.explanation: Appendix I reinforces PSR 040208, prohibiting officers from joining political parties or partisan events.

questions.question: What does PSR 040203 prohibit an officer from disclosing without authorization?
options: Information obtained in the course of official duty.
Annual reports issued through approved channels.
questions.explanation: PSR 040203 prohibits the unauthorized disclosure of information obtained in the course of official duty, including after retirement.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, the principle of impartiality under the **psr** is defined as?
options: Treating people differently based on status.
Equal treatment of all persons without bias or favour.
questions.explanation: PSR 040204 promotes impartiality, demanding equal and fair treatment of all without discrimination or bias.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, appendix i, integrity in public service is defined as?
options: Adhering to moral principles and honesty in official duties.
questions.explanation: Appendix I defines integrity as adherence to honesty, moral principles, and avoidance of corrupt practices Reference: Appendix I – Integrity.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 040105 states that every officer must discharge his duties with?
questions.explanation: PSR 040105 emphasizes honesty, impartiality and courtesy as essential values in discharging official duties.

questions.question: What action should be taken if an officer receives a gift from a contractor?
options: Return and report the incident immediately.
questions.explanation: PSR 040206(b) mandates that officers return and report gifts received from individuals having official dealings with them.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 040210, officers who become heavily indebted may face?
options: Disciplinary action for bringing disrepute to the Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 040210 provides that officers whose indebtedness brings embarrassment to the Service may be disciplined.

questions.question: When may a public officer maintain or operate a foreign bank account under Appendix I?
options: Operation of the account with personal presidential approval.
Operation of the account during official posting abroad.
Operation of the account after retirement.
Operation of the account on the basis of dual citizenship.
questions.explanation: Appendix I allows a public officer to maintain a foreign bank account only while the officer is on official posting abroad.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the officers are prohibited from accepting property or benefit of any kind from?
options: Private companies or individuals with official dealings.
questions.explanation: PSR 040206 and Appendix I prohibit officers from accepting gifts from individuals or entities having official relations with them.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule directs officers to display political neutrality in the discharge of duties?
questions.explanation: PSR 040208 provides that officers must remain politically neutral to maintain objectivity and public confidence.

questions.question: According to Appendix I, which behaviour violates integrity in public service
questions.explanation: Appendix I identifies bribery as a direct breach of the integrity principle in public service Reference: Appendix I – Integrity.

questions.question: **PSR** 040201 requires officers to uphold which of the following in performing duties
questions.explanation: PSR 040201 mandates that officers act with efficiency, honesty, and discipline at all times.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 040209, borrowing money from a subordinate is considered?
questions.explanation: PSR 040209 prohibits borrowing from subordinates or professional lenders; doing so constitutes serious misconduct.

questions.question: Who is responsible for ensuring officers in parastatals comply with the Code of Conduct?
questions.explanation: PSR 040103 assigns each Board responsibility to enforce the Code of Conduct within its organization.

questions.question: According to Appendix I, public officers must not engage in which of the following without approval
questions.explanation: Appendix I prohibits engaging in private business except farming or other activities approved by the Head of Service Reference: Appendix I – Private Business Restriction.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 040203 emphasizes that information obtained in official capacity should be?
questions.explanation: PSR 040203 directs officers to use official information only for authorized purposes and maintain confidentiality.

questions.question: Which rule prohibits officers from bringing the Service into disrepute through behaviour?
questions.explanation: PSR 040211 warns that behaviour bringing discredit to the Service constitutes misconduct punishable under PSR 100201.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, appendix i requires public officers to act with transparency, which is defined as?
options: Open and honest decision-making subject to accountability.
questions.explanation: Transparency in Appendix I requires openness, honesty, and accountability in decision-making processes Reference: Appendix I – Transparency.

questions.question: Which principle demands that public officers place public interest above personal gain?
questions.explanation: Appendix I emphasizes selflessness — putting public interest above private gain — as a core ethical value Reference: Appendix I – Selflessness.

questions.question: Under PSR 040107, what must an officer not engage in?
options: Tardiness as a separate attendance issue.
Any activity likely to conflict with official duties.
Volunteering under lawful approval in itself.
questions.explanation: PSR 040107 restricts officers from engaging in any outside activity that is likely to conflict with their official duties or responsibilities.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer who disobeys lawful instructions from a superior officer violates?
questions.explanation: PSR 040106 states that officers must obey all lawful instructions from superior officers promptly and efficiently.

questions.question: Which ethical principle is violated when an officer favours a relative in recruitment?
questions.explanation: Favouring relatives breaches impartiality as defined in PSR 040204 and Appendix I.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 040104 emphasizes that officers must show courtesy to?
options: Members of the public and colleagues alike.
questions.explanation: PSR 040104 requires courtesy and respect to both colleagues and members of the public.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the officers found guilty of corruption under **psr** may face?
questions.explanation: Under PSR 100201 and Appendix I, corruption constitutes serious misconduct punishable by dismissal and possible prosecution.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to appendix i, the ethical value of accountability implies that officers?
options: Must justify their decisions and actions to the public.
questions.explanation: Accountability in Appendix I means public officers must be answerable for their actions and decisions Reference: Appendix I – Accountability.

questions.question: What does PSR 040211 provide as the consequence of breaching the Code of Conduct?
options: Disciplinary measures as provided in the Public Service Rules.
Transfer or redeployment as the automatic response.
A reprimand without any formal record.
questions.explanation: PSR 040211 provides that breach of the Code of Conduct attracts disciplinary measures under the Public Service Rules, which may extend to serious sanctions depending on the case.

questions.question: Which rule emphasizes that public officers must refrain from actions detrimental to government image?
questions.explanation: PSR 040211 directs officers to avoid any action or speech that could discredit or embarrass the government.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer who misuses confidential information for personal gain violates?
questions.explanation: Misuse of official information for personal benefit breaches PSR 040203 and the integrity provisions in Appendix I.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 040101, officers must act in accordance with?
options: The Constitution, PSR and extant regulations.
questions.explanation: PSR 040101 requires all public officers to act in conformity with the Constitution, PSR and other extant laws.

questions.question: Which practice best strengthens governance in conduct and ethics administration?
options: Approved procedures with complete case records.
questions.explanation: Governance in conduct and ethics administration is strongest when officers follow approved procedures and keep complete records for review and accountability.

questions.question: For effective conduct & ethics, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure conduct & ethics compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk management in conduct and ethics administration?
options: Early escalation of material ethics-control exceptions.
questions.explanation: Risk management improves when material ethics-control exceptions are identified early, escalated promptly, and tracked for follow-up.

questions.question: When handling conduct & ethics, which choice reflects proper appointment governance standards?
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain disciplinary process in Conduct & Ethics?
options: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Conduct & Ethics, which action best demonstrates leave administration
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports promotion standards in conduct and ethics administration?
options: Promotion decisions handled through the approved standard.
Promotion decisions handled without the approved review process.
Personal preference used instead of the governing rule.
Convenience ahead of the applicable standard.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards are protected when decisions follow the approved rule and review path instead of informal preference.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on circular compliance within Conduct & Ethics?
options: Align practice with current circular directives and PSR rules.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Align practice with current circular directives and psr rules. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics.

questions.question: Which practice best supports documented procedure in conduct and ethics administration?
options: Complete records kept under the approved procedure.
questions.explanation: Documented procedure depends on following the approved process and keeping complete records of the steps taken.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Conduct & Ethics?
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which action best demonstrates public accountability in conduct and ethics administration?
questions.explanation: Public accountability depends on decisions that can be traced to recorded reasons and supporting evidence.

questions.question: For effective conduct & ethics, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk control in conduct and ethics administration?
options: Documented mitigation for identified ethics risks.
questions.explanation: Risk control is stronger when identified ethics risks are matched with documented mitigation and follow-up action.

questions.question: When handling conduct & ethics, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards?
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which routine best sustains operational discipline in conduct and ethics administration?
options: Approved workflow checks before case closure.
questions.explanation: Operational discipline depends on completing approved workflow checks before a matter is closed or advanced.

questions.question: In the context of Conduct & Ethics, which action best demonstrates citizen-focused service
options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports record management in conduct and ethics administration?
options: Current files with status updates at each control point.
questions.explanation: Record management depends on keeping files current and updating status at each control point so the matter remains reviewable.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on performance standards within Conduct & Ethics?
options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which routine best strengthens governance standards in conduct and ethics administration?
options: Approved governance procedures with complete records.
questions.explanation: Governance standards are strongest when officers follow the approved procedure consistently and keep the records needed for review and continuity.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain conduct & ethics compliance in Conduct & Ethics?
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Conduct & Ethics, which action best demonstrates conduct & ethics risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective conduct & ethics, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure appointment governance
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports disciplinary process in conduct and ethics administration?
options: Disclosure of conflicts with preservation of impartiality.
Preference for convenience over compliance requirements.
Known safeguards bypassed to save time.
questions.explanation: A proper disciplinary process depends on due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions rather than shortcuts or informal sanctions.

questions.question: When handling conduct & ethics, which choice reflects proper leave administration standards?
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports promotion standards in conduct and ethics cases?
options: Promotion decisions applied through the approved standard.
Promotion decisions handled without the approved review process.
Personal preference used instead of the governing rule.
Convenience ahead of the applicable standard.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards are sustained when the governing rule is applied consistently rather than being replaced by informal judgment.

questions.question: What kind of testimonial is required from applicants?
options: Good conduct from last employer or last school.
questions.explanation: A satisfactory testimonial from the last employer or last school is the standard character reference requested from applicants.

questions.question: Does the Oath of Secrecy prejudice the Freedom of Information Act?
questions.explanation: The Oath of Secrecy does not override the Freedom of Information Act; the two instruments operate within their own legal limits.

questions.question: A public officer is expected to demonstrate what towards the public service?
questions.explanation: Public officers are expected to show loyalty and commitment to the public service, because service to the state is the core duty of office.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the a public officer who receives a gift in the course of official duties must?
options: Declare it to the appropriate authority
questions.explanation: A gift received in the course of official duties must be declared to the appropriate authority so that the officer remains accountable and impartial.

questions.question: Which body ensures that public officers adhere to the provisions of the Code of Conduct?
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau monitors compliance with the Code of Conduct and helps ensure that public officers obey its provisions.

questions.question: Which constitutional document outlines the fundamental objectives of state policy, including ethical conduct?
questions.explanation: Chapter II of the 1999 Constitution contains the Fundamental Objectives and Directive Principles of State Policy, including the ethical ideals tested here.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the 'oath of allegiance' signifies commitment to?
questions.explanation: The Oath of Allegiance signifies commitment to the Constitution and the nation, not to a party or private interest.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the code of conduct aims to prevent officers from enriching themselves at the expense of?
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct is designed to prevent officers from enriching themselves at the expense of the state and the public.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the code of conduct is primarily designed to promote?
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct promotes good governance and public confidence by setting standards of ethical conduct for public officers.

questions.question: Which institution provides guidelines and training on ethical conduct?
questions.explanation: The Public Service Institute of Nigeria provides guidance and training on ethical conduct for public officers.

questions.question: Agency enforcing asset declaration rules?
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau is the constitutional body responsible for enforcing asset declaration rules.

questions.question: Prohibition on using official info for personal gain is in which instrument?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule to the Constitution contains the Code of Conduct provisions, including the prohibition on using official information for personal gain.

questions.question: Concept requiring regular disclosure of assets and liabilities?
questions.explanation: Regular disclosure of assets and liabilities is a Code of Conduct requirement that supports transparency and accountability.

questions.question: Core principle of public service ethics?
questions.explanation: Accountability and impartiality are core principles of public service ethics because they keep official conduct fair and answerable.

questions.question: Constitutional body collecting and verifying asset declarations?
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau is the constitutional body that collects and verifies asset declarations from public officers.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the main aim of the Oath of Secrecy required of some civil servants" according to official standards?
options: To hide personal assets from the public.
To protect government secrets and classified information.
To prevent officers from speaking to the media in all circumstances.
To ensure loyalty to a political party.
questions.explanation: The Oath of Secrecy is meant to protect official secrets and classified government information accessed by the officer in the course of duty.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the objective of the Oath of Secrecy" according to official standards?
options: Securing loyalty to a political party.
Hiding personal assets from the public.
Protection of government secrets and classified information.
Preventing all contact with the media.
questions.explanation: The Oath of Secrecy is intended to protect government secrets and classified information handled by officers in the course of duty.

questions.question: Every newly appointed officer or staff member is demand to sign which of the following?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Oath of Secrecy). Rule 020210 mandates that every newly appointed officer/staff member must sign an Oath of Secrecy on Security (Form 1).

questions.question: What fundamental principle of financial management is supported by the detailed accounting procedures for pensions in Chapter?
options: Transparency and accountability in public finance.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Transparency and accountability in public finance.). The detailed regulations, reconciliation requirements, and independent unit creation all support principles of transparency and accountability in managing pension funds. Part 4: Financial Regulation: Government Vehicles

questions.question: The objective of a "circular"?
options: To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.). A circular is used to communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.

questions.question: The role of a "circular"?
options: To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.
To provide a confidential report. Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large. 463). A circular is used to communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the aim of a circular" according to official standards?
options: To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to staff.
To issue a public press interview.
questions.explanation: A circular is used to communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to staff in a ministry or throughout the service.

questions.question: Which option best describes the objective of a "circular"?
options: To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.). A circular is used to communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large. 499

questions.question: Which feature of good governance means that decisions are taken according to rules and regulations and that information is openly available?
questions.explanation: Transparency in governance means that decisions and their enforcement follow laid-down rules and regulations while relevant information remains openly available and accessible.

questions.question: To which body should allegations that a public officer has breached the Code of Conduct be reported?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Allegations that a public officer has breached the Code of Conduct should be reported to the Code of Conduct Bureau, which receives and processes such complaints.

questions.question: Which officers are required to swear to the Oath of Secrecy?
options: Those who have access to classified information.
questions.explanation: Officers who have access to classified information should, as soon as possible, swear to the Oath of Secrecy.

subcategories.name: Medical & Welfare
subcategories.description: Medical examinations, welfare provisions, and service health matters.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 130101, what is the general policy on the medical treatment of public officers?
options: Officers are responsible for their own treatment.
Government provides medical attention in approved hospitals.
Treatment is only provided for senior officers.
questions.explanation: PSR 130101 provides that Government shall ensure medical attention for public officers and their families in approved medical facilities.

questions.question: Which category of officers is eligible for medical treatment at Government expense under **PSR** 2021?
options: All public officers and their dependants as defined in PSR 130102.
Procedurally, only directors and permanent secretaries within applicable policy conditions.
In practice, contract staff only in authorized administrative workflow.
Under formal rules, political appointees under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: PSR 130102 defines dependants and states that all public officers and their recognized dependants are entitled to medical treatment at Government expense.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 130104, who authorizes medical treatment outside Nigeria
options: President on the advice of a Medical Board.
questions.explanation: PSR 130104 stipulates that the President may approve treatment abroad based on the recommendation of a Medical Board.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 130106 provides that medical examination before appointment is?
options: Compulsory for all categories of appointments.
Required only for officers above GL.10.
questions.explanation: PSR 130106 mandates medical examination for all new entrants before assumption of duty to ensure fitness.

questions.question: When an officer is sick for more than 21 consecutive days, **PSR** 130107 requires that?
questions.explanation: PSR 130107 requires that any officer absent for more than 21 days due to illness must be examined by a Medical Board.

questions.question: Who certifies sick leave under **PSR** 130108?
options: Recognized Government or approved medical officer.
Private clinic of the officer’s choice.
questions.explanation: PSR 130108 states that sick leave must be supported by a certificate from a recognized Government medical officer.

questions.question: What is the maximum period of sick leave with full pay in **PSR** 130109?
questions.explanation: PSR 130109 provides that officers may be granted up to 180 days of sick leave with full pay upon certification by a Medical Board.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 130110 provides that if an officer is not fit after maximum sick leave, the next step is?
options: Extension without pay or retirement on medical grounds.
questions.explanation: PSR 130110 requires that if the officer is still unfit after maximum sick leave, the case shall be referred to the Medical Board for retirement or extension without pay.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 130111, what constitutes injury on duty
options: Any injury sustained during leisure time.
Injury sustained while performing official duties or as a direct consequence of it.
questions.explanation: PSR 130111 defines injury on duty as any injury occurring in the course of or arising directly from official duty.

questions.question: Compensation for injury or death in service is governed under which **PSR** rule
questions.explanation: PSR 130112 outlines procedures for compensation and benefits for injury, disability, or death in the line of duty.

questions.question: Who is responsible for verifying medical bills submitted for refund?
options: Medical Board or designated Government doctor.
questions.explanation: PSR 130105(b) requires medical bills to be verified by a Medical Board or designated Government doctor before reimbursement.

questions.question: What is the purpose of **PSR** 130113 on medical confidentiality?
options: To ensure privacy of officers' medical information.
To allow open disclosure of illness.
To publish medical data for transparency.
questions.explanation: PSR 130113 ensures that medical information about officers remains confidential and released only with authorization.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 130115 provides that officers posted to remote or hazardous areas may receive?
questions.explanation: PSR 130115 allows payment of hardship or hazard allowance to officers serving in difficult or high-risk areas.

questions.question: Medical examination for promotion or transfer is guided by which **PSR** provision
questions.explanation: PSR 130114 provides for medical examination in connection with promotion, transfer, or retirement as necessary.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule provides for reimbursement of expenses incurred on emergency treatment?
questions.explanation: PSR 130105 allows refund for approved emergency medical treatment when immediate attention is necessary.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 130107 states that where illness continues beyond 12 months, the officer shall?
options: Be deemed permanently unfit for service.
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(c) provides that illness exceeding 12 months indicates permanent unfitness for service and triggers medical retirement.

questions.question: What is the main the purpose of **psr** 130111–130113 is to ensure officers injured in service receive?
questions.explanation: PSR 130111–130113 provide for medical care, compensation, or pension benefits to officers injured in the course of duty.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the officers may be required to undergo periodic medical examination under **psr**?
questions.explanation: PSR 130114 empowers the Government to request periodic medical examinations to confirm officers’ continued fitness for duty.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 130112 ensures that dependants of an officer who dies in service are entitled to?
questions.explanation: PSR 130112(b) guarantees payment of compensation and death benefits to dependants of officers who die in service.

questions.question: What body determines the amount of compensation payable under **PSR** 130112?
questions.explanation: PSR 130112(c) provides that the Medical Board determines compensation based on degree of injury or disability.

questions.question: Under **PSR** 130101, what must officers do when reporting for medical treatment
options: Present their staff ID and medical registration card.
questions.explanation: PSR 130101(b) requires officers to present staff identification and medical registration card for treatment at government-approved hospitals.

questions.question: Who convenes the Medical Board for assessing prolonged illness?
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(b) provides that the Permanent Secretary convenes the Medical Board when an officer’s illness exceeds 21 days.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 130115 allows government to pay hazard allowance to officers working in?
options: Areas involving danger or health risk.
questions.explanation: PSR 130115 defines hazard allowance as payable to officers working in dangerous or high-risk environments.

questions.question: Which **PSR** provision covers treatment of dependants of officers?
questions.explanation: PSR 130102 clearly states that dependants of public officers are entitled to medical treatment at Government expense.

questions.question: What **PSR** rule governs the refund of medical expenses incurred in emergencies?
questions.explanation: PSR 130105 authorizes refund of medical expenses where immediate treatment was necessary and prior approval could not be obtained.

questions.question: Under PSR 130110, what may a Medical Board recommend when unfitness persists?
options: Extension of leave without pay or retirement on medical grounds.
questions.explanation: PSR 130110 allows a Medical Board to recommend extension of leave without pay or retirement on medical grounds where unfitness persists.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 130113, medical records of officers should be handled with?
questions.explanation: PSR 130113 requires confidentiality of officers' medical records, ensuring privacy except when disclosure is authorized.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 130107 stipulates that a medical board must be convened after illness lasts more than?
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(a) mandates that a Medical Board be convened when illness exceeds 21 consecutive days.

questions.question: What **PSR** provision authorizes periodic health check-ups for serving officers?
questions.explanation: PSR 130114 empowers the Government to arrange periodic medical examinations for officers to assess ongoing fitness for duty.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule allows the Government to provide insurance coverage for public officers?
questions.explanation: PSR 130116 provides that Government may arrange group life insurance policies to cover officers against death or permanent disability.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 130112, compensation claims must be supported by?
options: A written request from the officer.
Certified report by a Medical Board.
questions.explanation: PSR 130112(b) requires that all compensation claims be supported by certified Medical Board reports establishing injury or death in service.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the officers who refuse medical examination when directed may face?
questions.explanation: PSR 130114(b) provides that refusal to undergo mandatory medical examination constitutes misconduct under PSR 100201.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 130105, officers who seek medical attention in emergencies must?
options: Inform their Head of Department as soon as possible.
questions.explanation: PSR 130105(b) requires officers to notify their Head of Department promptly after emergency treatment for claim processing.

questions.question: When an officer dies as a result of injury sustained on duty, compensation is payable to?
options: The officer’s next of kin or dependants.
questions.explanation: PSR 130112(a) states that compensation shall be paid to the deceased officer’s next of kin or dependants.

questions.question: Which **PSR** provision restricts medical treatment abroad unless facilities are unavailable locally?
questions.explanation: PSR 130104 restricts treatment abroad to cases certified by a Medical Board as unmanageable within Nigeria.

questions.question: Who may authorize emergency medical expenditure refund in Ministries?
questions.explanation: PSR 130105(c) empowers the Accounting Officer (Permanent Secretary) to approve verified emergency medical refunds.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 130112(b) provides that compensation for permanent disability shall depend on?
options: Degree of disability assessed by Medical Board.
Recommendation from the Ministry of Finance.
questions.explanation: PSR 130112(b) states that the amount of compensation depends on the degree of disability certified by a Medical Board.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 130115, hazard allowance may be withdrawn when?
options: The hazardous condition ceases to exist.
questions.explanation: PSR 130115(b) states that hazard allowance ceases once the officer is no longer exposed to hazardous conditions.

questions.question: What document must be completed for an officer to claim compensation under **PSR** 130112?
questions.explanation: PSR 130112(a) requires completion of a Medical Board report form confirming injury or death before compensation can be processed.

questions.question: Under PSR 130111, when is an injury compensable?
options: Injury sustained during official duty or as its direct consequence.
Injury sustained at home in any circumstance.
questions.explanation: PSR 130111 treats an injury as compensable only when it is sustained during official duty or as a direct consequence of that duty.

questions.question: What **PSR** provision provides for medical treatment of dependants abroad in exceptional cases?
questions.explanation: PSR 130104(c) allows dependants' treatment abroad only when the President approves on medical advice.

questions.question: **PSR** 130107(a) requires medical review of prolonged illness after how many months
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(a) directs review of prolonged illness after six months for assessment by the Medical Board.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule ensures confidentiality of officers' medical conditions?
questions.explanation: PSR 130113 safeguards confidentiality of all medical records and reports of public officers.

questions.question: Who is responsible for maintaining officers’ health and safety at work under **PSR**'?
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: PSR 130115(b) assigns responsibility for health, safety, and welfare of officers to the Accounting Officer of each Ministry.

questions.question: When can an officer on sick leave be recalled to duty?
options: Only when a Medical Board certifies fitness to resume.
questions.explanation: PSR 130109(c) states that officers can resume duty only after being certified fit by a Government medical officer.

questions.question: Who approves retirement on medical grounds under **PSR** 130110?
questions.explanation: PSR 130110 provides that the Federal Civil Service Commission approves medical retirement upon recommendation of a Medical Board.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 130112, compensation is not payable if injury results from?
questions.explanation: PSR 130112(c) disallows compensation where injury or death results from the officer’s negligence or misconduct.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 130116 provides that insurance benefits shall cover?
options: Death, injury or permanent disability while in service.
Procedurally, salary advance within applicable policy conditions.
In practice, overtime payment in authorized administrative workflow.
Under formal rules, training reimbursement under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: PSR 130116 ensures insurance benefits cover death, injury, or permanent disability of officers during service.

questions.question: Under PSR 130104, when should treatment abroad be considered?
options: Treatment whenever the officer prefers foreign care.
Treatment only where adequate facilities remain unavailable in Nigeria.
Treatment whenever the officer can afford foreign hospitals.
Treatment whenever a superior officer requests it.
questions.explanation: PSR 130104 limits treatment abroad to cases where adequate treatment is unavailable in Nigeria and the case is properly certified.

questions.question: What is the primary the objective of **psr** chapter 13 is to ensure?
options: Efficient health and welfare support for public officers.
questions.explanation: PSR Chapter 13 aims to guarantee effective medical care, compensation, and welfare of public officers to sustain productivity.

questions.question: Which practice best strengthens governance in medical and welfare administration?
options: Approved procedures with complete case records.
questions.explanation: Governance in medical and welfare administration is strongest when officers follow the approved procedure and keep complete records for review and accountability.

questions.question: For effective medical & welfare, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure medical & welfare compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk management in medical and welfare administration?
options: Early escalation of material welfare-case exceptions.
questions.explanation: Risk management improves when material exceptions are identified early, escalated promptly, and tracked for follow-up.

questions.question: When handling medical & welfare, which choice reflects proper appointment governance standards?
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain disciplinary process in Medical & Welfare?
options: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Medical & Welfare, which action best demonstrates leave administration
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports promotion standards in medical and welfare administration?
options: Promotion decisions handled through the approved standard.
Promotion decisions handled without the approved review process.
Personal preference used instead of the governing rule.
Convenience ahead of the applicable standard.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards are protected when decisions follow the approved rule and review path instead of informal preference.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on circular compliance within Medical & Welfare?
options: Align practice with current circular directives and PSR rules.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Align practice with current circular directives and psr rules. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare.

questions.question: Which practice best supports documented procedure in medical and welfare administration?
options: Complete records kept under the approved procedure.
questions.explanation: Documented procedure depends on following the approved process and keeping complete records of the steps taken.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Medical & Welfare?
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which action best demonstrates public accountability in medical and welfare administration?
questions.explanation: Public accountability depends on decisions that can be traced to recorded reasons and supporting evidence.

questions.question: For effective medical & welfare, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk control in medical and welfare administration?
options: Documented mitigation for identified welfare-case risks.
questions.explanation: Risk control is stronger when identified risks are matched with documented mitigation and follow-up action.

questions.question: When handling medical & welfare, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards?
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which routine best sustains operational discipline in medical and welfare administration?
options: Approved workflow checks before case closure.
questions.explanation: Operational discipline depends on completing approved workflow checks before a matter is closed or advanced.

questions.question: In the context of Medical & Welfare, which action best demonstrates citizen-focused service
options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports record management in medical and welfare administration?
options: Current files with status updates at each control point.
questions.explanation: Record management depends on keeping files current and updating status at each control point so the matter remains reviewable.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on performance standards within Medical & Welfare?
options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which routine best strengthens governance standards in medical and welfare administration?
options: Approved governance procedures with complete records.
questions.explanation: Governance standards are strongest when officers follow the approved procedure consistently and keep the records needed for review and continuity.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain medical & welfare compliance in Medical & Welfare?
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Medical & Welfare, which action best demonstrates medical & welfare risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective medical & welfare, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure appointment governance
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports disciplinary process in medical and welfare administration?
options: Prompt documentation of clinical decisions with preserved records.
Preference for convenience over compliance requirements.
Known safeguards bypassed to save time.
questions.explanation: A proper disciplinary process depends on due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions rather than shortcuts or informal sanctions.

questions.question: When handling medical & welfare, which choice reflects proper leave administration standards?
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in medical & welfare Reference: Medical & Welfare - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports promotion standards in medical and welfare cases?
options: Promotion decisions applied through the approved standard.
Promotion decisions handled without the approved review process.
Personal preference used instead of the governing rule.
Convenience ahead of the applicable standard.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards are sustained when the governing rule is applied consistently rather than being replaced by informal judgment.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the entitlement of an officer on transfer on promotion" according to official standards?
options: The officer is entitled to the salary of the lower grade.
The officer is entitled to the salary of the new grade.
The officer is not entitled to any promotion benefits.
The officer is placed on a probationary period in the new grade. □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The officer is entitled to the salary of the new grade.). Rule 020506 specifies that an applicant must be considered for transfer-on-promotion to any MDA, except in response to an advertised post46. 26

questions.question: Which option best describes a duty of the Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC)?
options: Serving as an appellate body for petitions from officers who feel aggrieved on appointment, promotion, and discipline matters.
Managing the day-to-day operations of Ministries.
Approving the budget of MDAs. Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Serving as an appellate body for petitions from officers who feel aggrieved on appointment, promotion, and discipline matters.). The FCSC serves as an appellate body in respect of petitions from Officers in the MDAs who feel aggrieved concerning appointment, promotion, and discipline matters.

questions.question: What may happen when a non-pensionable officer causes pecuniary damage through negligence or failure to obey an order?
options: A salary deduction may be made to recover the damage.
The officer must be terminated immediately.
The full amount must be paid upfront in every case.
No liability arises from the damage.
questions.explanation: Where a non-pensionable officer causes pecuniary damage through negligence or failure to comply with an order, recovery may be made through salary deduction.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the main objective of compulsory examinations for police and paramilitary officers" according to official standards?
options: Officer familiarity with service rules, regulations, and laws.
questions.explanation: The compulsory examinations are intended to ensure that officers are conversant with the rules, regulations, and laws governing their service.

questions.question: The only objective for which a withdrawal can be made from a ministry's revenue account?
options: To make transfers to the Consolidated Revenue Fund (CRF) account.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To make transfers to the Consolidated Revenue Fund (CRF) account). Financial Regulation 213(ii) strictly states that on no account must any withdrawal be made from the revenue account other than for the aim of transfer to the consolidated account.

questions.question: Which role is liable to meet the cost of altering the lock and providing new keys if a key to a Government safe is lost due to negligence?
options: The officer responsible for the loss.
The supplier of the safe. Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The officer in charge for the loss.). Financial Regulation 1112 states, "The officer in charge for the loss of a key to a Government safe, is liable to be called upon to meet the cost of altering the lock and the provision of new keys, if found negligent."

questions.question: Can receipt and licence books be transferred between Sub-Accounting Officers?
options: Transfer with the consent of the Accounting Officer or in special emergencies with reporting.
Transfer restricted to unused books only.
Transfer at any time without approval.
questions.explanation: Receipt and licence books may be transferred with the consent of the Accounting Officer, or in special emergency cases provided the circumstances are reported.

questions.question: How often must the Public Service Rules (PSR) be reviewed?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Every five years.). The Preamble, under "Review of the Public Service Rules," specifies that "The Public Service Rules (PSR) must be reviewed every five years..."

questions.question: What does "cut-off mark" mean in the context of the PSR?
options: The minimum score for passing any exam.
The maximum score achievable in an examination.
The score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies.). Rule 010105 defines "cut-off mark" as "the score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies."

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the maximum total duration of a contract appointment under Rule 020406(vi)" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: Rule 020406(vi) states that a contract appointment may be for one or two years and may be renewed, but it must not exceed four years in total. The maximum total duration is therefore four years.

questions.question: How is "transfer" defined in the Public Service Rules?
options: The temporary release of an officer to the service of another government or international body.
The permanent release of an officer from one scheduled Service to another or from one class to another within the same Service.
The movement of an officer to a new post on promotion. □.
The movement of an officer from one ministry to another.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The permanent release of an officer from one scheduled Service to another or from one class to another within the same Service.). Rule 020501 defines transfer as "the permanent release of an officer from one scheduled service to another or from one class to another within the same service."

questions.question: An officer applying for an inter-service transfer or secondment to a post on GL. 07-10 is demand to sit for which examination?
options: The Annual Competitive Civil Service Entry Examination.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Annual Competitive Civil Service Entry Examination). Rule 020503(ii) states that officers seeking a transfer from scheduled establishments on GL. 07-10 are necessitate to "sit for the Annual Competitive Civil Service Entry Examination.

questions.question: During secondment, what happens to an officer's substantive post and promotion prospects?
options: The substantive post is held and promotion prospects are maintained.
The officer is permanently transferred to the receiving organization.
The substantive post is frozen and promotion is suspended.
The substantive post and promotion prospects are forfeited.
questions.explanation: During secondment, the officer's substantive post continues to be held and the officer remains entitled to increment and promotion under the governing rule.

questions.question: Which option best describes the definition of "posting" according to the Public Service Rules?
options: The movement of an officer on promotion. □.
The initial assignment of an officer in a position/job schedule in an MDA.
The permanent transfer of an officer to a new location.
The temporary movement of an officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The initial assignment of an officer in a position/job schedule in an MDA.). Rule 020601 defines posting as "the initial assignment of an officer in a position/job schedule in an MDA, or reassignment to another position/job schedule within or outside his current MDA/Department."

questions.question: The principle of posting is intended to guarantee that the system is not used as a tool for what?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Punishment or favoritism.). Rule 020603 emphasizes that posting is done to guarantee fairness to all officers and that the system is not used as a tool for "coercion, punishment, or favoritism."

questions.question: The authority accountable for the promotion of all posts in the Federal Civil Service, except for Permanent Secretaries?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Federal Civil Service Commission). Rule 020801(a) states that promotion to all posts in the Federal Civil Service, other than those of Permanent Secretaries, is vested in the Federal Civil Service Commission.

questions.question: How many years must an officer on GL 06 and below spend on a post before being considered for promotion?
questions.explanation: Rule 020802(c) states that an officer on GL 06 and below must spend a minimum of two years on a post before being considered for promotion. The period being tested is therefore two years.

questions.question: How is promotion to all posts made?
options: Strictly on the basis of competitive merit from amongst all eligible candidates □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Strictly on the basis of competitive merit from amongst all eligible candidates □ 33). Rule 020802(d) states that promotion must be made "strictly on the basis of competitive merit from amongst all eligible candidates."

questions.question: The promotion of officers on GL. 07-14 is subject to confirmation by which body?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The promotion committee of the MDA. □.
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Federal Civil Service Commission/Board). Rule 020802(e)(ii) states that promotion on GL 07-14 is subject to "confirmation by the Federal Civil Service Commission/Board.

questions.question: Which option aligns with the meaning of the effective date of a promotion?
options: The 1st of January of the year in which the promotion exercise was conducted or as may be determined by the Federal Civil Service Commission.
The date determined by the Permanent Secretary
The date of the promotion exercise
The date the officer assumes duty in the new post a
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The 1st of January of the year in which the promotion exercise was conducted or as may be determined by the Federal Civil Service Commission.). Rule 020806 states that the effective date of promotion "must be 1st January of the year for which the promotion exercise was conducted or as may be determined by the Federal Civil Service Commission."

questions.question: Responsible for compiling a list of all eligible candidates before each promotion exercise?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The appropriate office). Rule 020803 states that before each promotion exercise, the appropriate office "must compile a list of all eligible candidates to be considered."

questions.question: How is the promotion of a contract officer handled?
options: Advancement based only on long years of service.
Consideration for an enhanced appointment during contract renegotiation.
Placement on a separate routine promotion list.
questions.explanation: A contract officer is not promoted in the ordinary sense; instead, the officer may be considered for an enhanced appointment when the contract is renegotiated.

subcategories.name: Training, Performance & Career Development
subcategories.description: Training obligations, performance, and progression pathways.

questions.question: What is the main according to **psr** 070101, the purpose of training in the public service is to?
options: Enhance efficiency and effectiveness in service delivery.
questions.explanation: PSR 070101 provides that the purpose of training is to develop officers’ efficiency, effectiveness, and performance in public service delivery.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 070102 emphasizes that training should be based on?
questions.explanation: PSR 070102 states that all training must be guided by manpower requirements and organizational development plans.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 070104, the head of service is responsible for?
options: Issuing training circulars and guidelines for the Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 070104 designates the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation to issue policy guidelines and coordinate training across MDAs.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 070105, every ministry, department, or agency must have a?
questions.explanation: PSR 070105 mandates that every MDA establish a Training and Staff Development Committee to oversee training programs.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 070107 requires officers to undergo training relevant to?
options: Their immediate duties and future responsibilities.
questions.explanation: PSR 070107 emphasizes that officers should undertake training related to their present and anticipated job roles.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 050102, performance appraisal should be based on?
options: Measured results, outputs, and behavioural competencies.
