questions.explanation: PSR 110116 provides that official conversations or decisions made by phone must be recorded or noted in writing for record purposes.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 110117, misplacement of official files is regarded as?
questions.explanation: PSR 110117 classifies misplacement or loss of official files as negligence and misconduct subject to disciplinary action.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to psr 110118, government vehicles and equipment shall be used only for?
options: The vehicles and equipment are used only for official purposes.
The vehicles and equipment are used for personal errands when official work is done.
The vehicles and equipment are used for emergencies at night.
The vehicles and equipment are used with supervisor's informal consent.
questions.explanation: PSR 110118 bars personal use of government vehicles and equipment, so the correct rule is that they are reserved for official purposes only.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under psr 110119, the head of a department is responsible for ensuring that?
options: The department head ensures that government property and records are properly maintained.
The department head ensures that staff arrive early to work.
The department head ensures that only senior officers sign correspondence.
The department head ensures that all staff attend weekly meetings.
questions.explanation: PSR 110119 makes the department head responsible for maintaining government property and official records, so the control duty sits at the departmental level.

questions.question: According to PSR 110120, between what hours must government offices remain open to the public?
questions.explanation: PSR 110120 provides that government offices are open to the public between 8:00 am and 4:00 pm, Monday to Friday, except on public holidays.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 110121, late arrival to duty without permission is considered?
questions.explanation: PSR 110121 classifies lateness and unauthorized absence as negligence of duty and a disciplinary offence.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110122 requires that staff attendance be recorded in?
questions.explanation: PSR 110122 directs that all Ministries maintain an attendance register for daily record of officers’ presence at work.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 110123, officers are required to report for duty?
questions.explanation: PSR 110123 requires punctual attendance and adherence to official hours of duty by all officers.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110124 provides that officers who need to leave duty before closing time must?
options: Sign the movement register with permission.
Send a text message to supervisor.
questions.explanation: PSR 110124 requires officers to obtain permission and sign the movement register before leaving duty before closing hours.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 110125, when an officer is absent without permission for ten consecutive days, it shall be treated as?
questions.explanation: PSR 110125 deems absence without permission for ten working days as resignation from service with forfeiture of benefits.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110126 provides that all ministries must ensure adequate?
questions.explanation: PSR 110126 emphasizes proper filing systems and control of records to ensure administrative efficiency.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 110127, officers responsible for mail handling must?
options: Keep proper registers of incoming and outgoing mails.
questions.explanation: PSR 110127 mandates proper recordkeeping of all incoming and outgoing correspondence through designated mail registers.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110128 states that officers must use official email and correspondence channels to?
questions.explanation: PSR 110128 encourages use of official email systems to ensure record traceability and prevent data breaches.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 110129, officers responsible for registry work must be trained in?
questions.explanation: PSR 110129 directs that registry officers should be trained in modern records and information management for efficiency.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110130 provides that all correspondences should be signed and dated to?
questions.explanation: PSR 110130 emphasizes signing and dating of documents to ensure authenticity and accountability.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 110131, officers must not remove official documents from office without?
questions.explanation: PSR 110131 prohibits unauthorized removal of government documents except with written authorization from a superior officer.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110132 provides that minutes of official meetings must be?
options: Filed, numbered, and preserved for reference.
questions.explanation: PSR 110132 requires that all minutes of official meetings be properly filed and preserved for audit and reference.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110133 provides that reports or returns requested by superior authorities must be?
questions.explanation: PSR 110133 mandates that reports requested by higher authorities be submitted accurately and without delay.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 110134, waste of government stationery and materials is classified as?
questions.explanation: PSR 110134 considers waste or careless use of government stationery as negligence requiring disciplinary action.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110135 requires that all ministries keep?
questions.explanation: PSR 110135 requires each Ministry to maintain a Visitors’ Book to record all official visits and inquiries.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 110136, officers responsible for stores and inventory must?
options: Keep stock ledgers and update balances regularly.
questions.explanation: PSR 110136 requires store officers to maintain up-to-date stock ledgers for accountability and audit purposes.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110137 provides that confidential records should be accessed only by?
questions.explanation: PSR 110137 stipulates that access to confidential records is restricted to duly authorized officers only.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 110138, misuse of official information is punishable as?
questions.explanation: PSR 110138 classifies misuse or unauthorized disclosure of official information as serious misconduct.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110139 directs that records of staff discipline and performance be?
questions.explanation: PSR 110139 mandates proper filing of disciplinary and performance records in officers’ personal files for reference.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 110140, annual reports from ministries should reach the head of service by?
options: End of January following the year under review.
questions.explanation: PSR 110140 provides that annual reports must be submitted to the Head of Service by the end of January following the review year.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110141 emphasizes that administrative instructions shall be?
questions.explanation: PSR 110141 directs that all administrative instructions must be put in writing to maintain accountability and documentation.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 110142 provides that obsolete files may be destroyed after?
options: Approval of the authorized officer and proper record.
questions.explanation: PSR 110142 mandates that obsolete files may only be destroyed after approval of an authorized officer and documentation.

questions.question: What is the main the general purpose of chapter 11 of the **psr** is to?
options: Ensure effective administrative coordination and record integrity.
questions.explanation: Chapter 11 of the PSR ensures proper administrative coordination, accountability, and efficient record management across the Civil Service.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **psr** requires that where the head of a department is not available for a period spanning one month (due to approved assignment or leave), the most senior officer is mandated to oversee the office of the head of the department. this is covered by?
questions.explanation: Where the Head of a Department/Division/Unit/Branch is not available for a period spanning one month.. the most senior Officer in the Department or Organization shall be mandated to oversee the Office of the Head of the Department (PSR 020708(b)).

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **psr** provides that an officer may be granted **leave of absence** on grounds of public policy. the leave shall be approved by?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: Leave of Absence on grounds of public policy shall be approved by the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).

questions.question: Under PSR Rule 120226, which type of study leave allows an officer to continue receiving salary while studying?
questions.explanation: PSR Rule 120226 lists study leave with pay as one of the recognized forms of study leave. It is the option that allows the officer to continue receiving salary during the approved study period.

questions.question: Under PSR Rule 120226, which type of study leave does not carry salary during the approved study period?
questions.explanation: PSR Rule 120226 recognizes study leave without pay as one of the three types of study leave. It is the form granted when the officer is approved to study without continuing salary during that period.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the final authority responsible for the appointment, promotion, and discipline of directors (**gl 17**) in the federal civil service is the?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The FCSC is vested with powers to appoint, promote, and discipline civil servants up to certain grade levels, including the Directorate Cadre.

questions.question: Under **PSR Rule 100429**, an officer on leave of absence must obtain express approval before engaging in what activity?
options: Further studies related to the service.
questions.explanation: Rule 100429 requires an officer on leave of absence to obtain express approval before accepting any paid employment.

questions.question: What is the maximum period of sabbatical leave permitted for an officer on **GL 15** and above (**PSR** 120223)?
questions.explanation: Sabbatical leave applies to officers on GL 15 and above for a period of twelve (12) calendar months once in five (5) years.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule allows for the **Sabbatical Appointment** of an officer on **GL 15** and above?
questions.explanation: Rule 020413 applies Sabbatical Appointment for the purpose of research and professional development of the Officer.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the consequence when an officer fails to meet the minimum years required in a post before promotion eligibility" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: Failing to meet the minimum tenure requirement makes the officer ineligible for the promotion exercise until the required period in post is completed.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a category of Leave of Absence on grounds of public policy (**PSR** 120236)?
options: Leave of Absence to join spouse on course of instruction of not less than 9 months duration abroad.
Leave for compassionate grounds (e.g., burial of spouse).
Special Leave of Absence to join spouse on grounds of public policy.
Leave of Absence on grounds of public policy for Technical Aid Programmes.
questions.explanation: Leave for compassionate grounds (PSR 120233) is a type of leave but is separate from Leave of Absence on grounds of public policy (PSR 120236).

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the **psr** **rule 120235** prohibits an officer on **leave of absence** from accepting?
questions.explanation: An officer on leave of absence shall not accept any paid employment without previously obtaining the express approval of the OHCSF (PSR 120235).

questions.question: What is the maximum number of days a newly appointed Officer on **GL.07**–17 is expected to complete the confirmation examination after taking up their appointment in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office?
questions.explanation: All officers must take the confirmation examination within two years from the date of taking up their appointments in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office Reference: Confirmation Examination.

questions.question: Which institution provides training and capacity development support for civil servants for career advancement beyond **GL 10**'?
questions.explanation: Officers beyond GL 10 shall attend ASCON, PSIN, or other approved institutions (Rule 030501(d)).

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule strictly prohibits an officer from seeking the influence of prominent persons for obtaining consideration in matters connected with promotion or postings?
questions.explanation: Rule 100427 prohibits officers from seeking undue influence from prominent persons regarding official matters like promotion or discipline.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the the final authority for ratifying amendments to the public service rules (**psr**), based on recommendations from the national council on establishments (nce), is the?
options: Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The FEC gives final ratification to PSR amendments, following the review process coordinated by the OHCSF.

questions.question: Which leave types, in addition to joining a spouse on grounds of public policy, are listed under the three leave-of-absence categories in Rule 120236?
options: Leave to join a spouse on grounds of public policy only.
questions.explanation: Rule 120236 includes leave of absence to join a spouse on grounds of public policy, special leave to join a spouse on course of instruction, and leave for Technical Aid Programmes.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the unauthorized use of government transport facilities or allowances by an officer is classified under the **psr** as?
questions.explanation: Misuse of free transport or allowances is categorized as Serious Misconduct (Rule 080106).

questions.question: For effective general administration & office procedures, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure general administration & office procedures compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in general administration & office procedures Reference: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which action best supports public-service compliance in general administration and office procedures?
options: Compliance with approved procedures supported by complete records.
Bypassing of review and approval checkpoints to save time.
Preference for convenience over compliance requirements.
Inconsistent application of rules across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Public-service compliance is strongest when control gaps are identified early and material exceptions are escalated before they become harder to correct.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: When handling general administration & office procedures, which choice reflects proper appointment governance standards?
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in general administration & office procedures Reference: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain disciplinary process in General Administration & Office Procedures?
options: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in general administration & office procedures Reference: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the context of General Administration & Office Procedures, which action best demonstrates leave administration
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in general administration & office procedures Reference: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the most appropriate approach to promotion standards in general administration and office procedures" according to official standards?
options: Eligibility confirmation before recommendation for advancement.
Preference for convenience over compliance requirements.
Bypassing of review and approval checkpoints to save time.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards are upheld when eligibility is checked before advancement is recommended, keeping the process fair and consistent with the rules.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on circular compliance within General Administration & Office Procedures?
options: Align practice with current circular directives and PSR rules.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Align practice with current circular directives and psr rules. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in general administration & office procedures.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which action best preserves compliance and service quality in general administration and office procedures?
options: Use of credible official sources with confirmation of facts before conclusions.
Preference for convenience over compliance requirements.
questions.explanation: Compliance and service quality are preserved when PSR provisions are applied consistently and the record remains auditable throughout the process.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the "classified correspondence" is correspondence that has been graded?
options: Restricted, Confidential, Secret or Top Secret
questions.explanation: Classified correspondence is graded Restricted, Confidential, Secret, or Top Secret to control access.

questions.question: Which form shall be used for staff records?
questions.explanation: Gen 60 is the standard staff record form used to capture employee service details.

questions.question: Where must a copy of the Staff Record Form (Gen 60) be sent?
options: National Record Centre (OHCSF), Accountant-General, Auditor-General, and PenCom.
questions.explanation: Copies of Gen 60 are sent to the National Record Centre and the listed oversight offices so the records remain centralized.

questions.question: Within what time frame should the Staff Record Form (Gen 60) be sent?
options: Within one month of the appointment.
questions.explanation: The form must be sent within one month of appointment so the record is created promptly.

questions.question: Why is record-keeping emphasized in CSH?
questions.explanation: Record-keeping is emphasized because it supports accountability and continuity across postings and administrations.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "a 'dispatch book' in registry practice" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: A dispatch book is the registry record used to track outgoing official correspondence, so it serves as the ledger for documents leaving the office.

questions.question: Choose the best answer for: How can physical proximity and accessibility of services be achieved?
options: By reducing the number of services offered. □.
By the application of appropriate information and communication technologies (E-Governance)IO.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (By the application of appropriate information and communication technologies (E-Governance)IO.). The handbook states that physical proximity and accessibility can be achieved by the application of appropriate information and communication technologies (E-Governance).
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the purpose of a secret registry" according to official standards?
options: To keep classified documents and correspondence.
To keep records of all open meetings.
questions.explanation: A secret registry exists to receive, control, and safeguard classified documents and correspondence that require restricted handling.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the purpose of classifying a document as confidential" according to official standards?
options: To protect information that could be harmful if disclosed.
To make it a permanent document.
To prevent every other ministry from ever seeing it.
To make it difficult to access for no reason.
questions.explanation: A confidential classification is meant to protect sensitive information from unauthorized disclosure, especially where disclosure could harm official work or public interest.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Required for staff on GL. 03 -13 on arrival at the office and at the close of work?
options: They must send an email to their supervisor.
They must report to their superior officer.
They must sign the Attendance Register.
They must punch a time clock. a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They must sign the Attendance Register..). All staff on GL. 03-13 are demand to sign the Attendance Register on arrival at the Office in the morning as well as at close of work at the end of the day..
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: What handling rule follows when a document is classified as confidential?
options: To protect sensitive information from unauthorized disclosure.
To grant unrestricted access to the document.
questions.explanation: Once a document is classified as confidential, access and disclosure must be limited to authorized officers who need the information for official duty.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which office is must swear to the Oath of Secrecy?
options: Those who have access to classified information.
questions.explanation: Only officers with access to classified information are required to swear the Oath of Secrecy.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: In a Secret Registry, what is typically kept?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Only highly classified subject files.). Where the registry is split into two, only highly classified subject files are kept in the Secret Registry whilst the Confidential Registry keeps the confidential personal files of the staff.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which description best matches a subject file in office records management?
options: Documents relating to a staff member's personal life.
The personal assets of a public officer.
Financial records of the government in general.
Correspondence and documents relating to a particular subject matter.
questions.explanation: In office records management, a subject file is a file containing official correspondence and related documents on a particular subject matter.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: What material should never be used for drafting, especially when classified information is involved?
options: Clean recycled paper without classified content.
Plain notebooks kept for approved office use.
questions.explanation: Discarded papers containing classified information should never be reused for drafting because they may expose sensitive content and compromise document security.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: What form must be used for an officer's staff document, and how many copies must be sent to the National document Centre?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Gen 60, one copy). According to Rule 020107(i), a Staff document Form (Gen 60) must be used, and one copy must be sent to the National document Centre.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: The authority to procedure inter-service transfer and secondment for posts graded GL. 06 and below lies with which body?
options: The appropriate Committees of the benefitting Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The appropriate Committees of the benefitting Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices.). Rule 020502 states that applications for transfer/secondment to posts on GL. 06 and below must be considered by the appropriate committees of the benefitting ministries/extra-ministerial offices.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: The Chairman of the Examination Board for the confirmation examinations must be of what rank?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Director). Rule 030103 states that the Examination Board must be headed by a Director
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: The handbook states that every Civil Servant should avoid making a living from "touting". What does "touting" refer to in this context?
options: Working a second job outside of the Civil Service. E3.
Earning a living from official matters, like processing files for people.
Giving out official information to the public.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Earning a living from official matters, like processing files for people.). The practice of making a living from "touting" for official matters, by charging people to workflow their files, is a disgraceful and corrupt act.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: What should administrative units make available to the public?
options: Necessary information on the unit's acts and procedures.
No information on the ground of universal classification.
Only information already published in newspapers.
questions.explanation: Administrative units should make necessary information on their acts and procedures available so the public can understand how the unit is managed and how to engage it properly.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Why must the public service establish or strengthen reception and information units for users?
options: To help users access services and record their views, suggestions, or complaints.
To file lawsuits against the government.
To encourage complaints without offering assistance.
questions.explanation: Reception and information units help users gain access to services and provide channels for recording their views, suggestions, and complaints as part of responsive public administration.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: If an officer is charged with a criminal offense, to whom must the officer promptly report it?
options: Prompt report to the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
Prompt report to the National Assembly.
Prompt report to the Central Bank.
questions.explanation: The PSR requires an officer charged with a criminal offence to report promptly to the Permanent Secretary or the Head of the relevant Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who has overall responsibility for monitoring PSR implementation and compliance across all MDAs?
options: The Office of the Head of Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation monitors implementation and compliance with the PSR.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Do the eligibility rules specify a minimum number of years of service for appointment as Permanent Secretary?
options: No fixed minimum number of years stated.
questions.explanation: The eligibility rules do not state a fixed minimum number of years of service for appointment as Permanent Secretary.
questions.chapter: General Administration & Office Procedures - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the according to **psr** 180101, the public service rules 2021 came into effect on?
questions.explanation: PSR 180101 provides that the Public Service Rules 2021 took effect from 27th July 2021 as approved by the President.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the **psr** 180102 provides that interpretation of any rule shall be the responsibility of?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: PSR 180102 vests interpretation authority in the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 180103, in the event of conflict between **psr** and an establishment circular, precedence shall be given to?
questions.explanation: PSR 180103 provides that where a conflict arises, the provisions of the Public Service Rules prevail over any circular or instruction.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 180104, 'commission' refers to?
questions.explanation: PSR 180104 defines 'Commission' as the Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180105, 'permanent secretary' is defined as?
options: The administrative head of a Ministry or extra-ministerial department.
A staff of any grade level.
questions.explanation: PSR 180105 defines 'Permanent Secretary' as the administrative head of a Ministry or extra-ministerial office.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the meaning of 'officer' under PSR 180106" according to official standards?
options: Political office holder in public administration.
Person employed in a civil capacity in the Public Service of the Federation.
Member of the armed forces of the Federation.
Contractor or consultant engaged by government.
questions.explanation: PSR 180106 defines an officer as a person employed in a civil capacity in the Public Service of the Federation.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 180107, 'ministry' includes?
options: Any Federal Ministry or extra-ministerial office.
questions.explanation: PSR 180107 clarifies that 'Ministry' includes any Federal Ministry or extra-ministerial office under the Federal Government.

questions.question: According to PSR 180108, what does the term 'President' refer to?
options: Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
President and Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
Chairman of the Federal Civil Service Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 180108 uses 'President' to refer to the President and Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

questions.question: Under PSR 180109, what does the Commission's power include?
options: Recruitment, promotion, and discipline of officers.
questions.explanation: PSR 180109 provides that the Commission exercises powers over the appointment, promotion, and discipline of officers. The correct option therefore states those three core powers.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 180110, the phrase 'head of civil service' refers to?
options: The administrative head of the entire Federal Civil Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 180110 defines the Head of Civil Service as the administrative head of the Federal Civil Service and custodian of the Rules.

questions.question: What does 'Public Service' include under PSR 180111?
options: Only ministries and parastatals under normal usage.
Federal ministries, departments, and agencies under the executive arm.
questions.explanation: PSR 180111 includes Federal Ministries, Departments, and Agencies under the executive arm in the meaning of Public Service.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180112, 'serious misconduct' is defined as?
options: Specific act that can lead to dismissal.
questions.explanation: PSR 180112 defines 'serious misconduct' as a specific act that can lead to dismissal from service.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180113 provides that 'misconduct' is defined as?
questions.explanation: PSR 180113 defines 'misconduct' as failure to perform duties properly or violation of established rules and regulations.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, under **psr** 180114, 'permanent and pensionable appointment' is defined as?
options: An appointment that qualifies the holder for pension benefits.
questions.explanation: PSR 180114 defines a permanent and pensionable appointment as one which qualifies the holder for pension benefits after confirmation.

questions.question: Under PSR 180115, what is a temporary appointment?
options: An employment with no pension rights until confirmation.
questions.explanation: PSR 180115 describes a temporary appointment as employment without pension rights until the officer is confirmed. The correct option states that defining feature.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180116, 'salary' is defined as?
options: All approved emoluments including allowances and increments.
questions.explanation: PSR 180116 defines salary as all approved emoluments including basic pay, allowances, and approved increments.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180117 states that 'head of department' is defined as?
options: An officer in charge of a recognized department or division in a Ministry.
questions.explanation: PSR 180117 defines 'Head of Department' as the officer responsible for a department or division recognized by the Government.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180118 provides that 'confirmation of appointment' is defined as?
options: Formal acceptance of satisfactory service after probation.
questions.explanation: PSR 180118 defines confirmation as formal recognition of satisfactory completion of probation.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 180119, the term 'minister' refers to?
options: A member of the Federal Executive Council in charge of a Ministry.
questions.explanation: PSR 180119 defines 'Minister' as a member of the Federal Executive Council responsible for a Ministry.

questions.question: According to PSR 180120, what does 'Extra-Ministerial Office' mean?
options: Private organization carrying out government business.
Federal Government office that does not form part of a ministry.
questions.explanation: PSR 180120 defines an Extra-Ministerial Office as a Federal Government office that does not form part of a ministry.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the **psr** 180121 clarifies that 'commissioner' in the context of the rules refers to?
options: Federal Commissioner in the Civil Service Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 180121 defines 'Commissioner' as a member of the Federal Civil Service Commission.

questions.question: Under PSR 180122, what is probation?
options: A temporary period for assessment before confirmation.
questions.explanation: PSR 180122 describes probation as a temporary period during which an officer is assessed before confirmation of appointment. The correct option matches that assessment period.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, under **psr** 180123, 'promotion' is defined as?
options: Elevation to a higher grade level with greater responsibility.
questions.explanation: PSR 180123 defines promotion as the advancement of an officer to a higher post with increased responsibility.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 180124 states that 'transfer' refers to?
options: Movement between posts of equal grade level and status.
Procedurally, dismissal within applicable policy conditions.
In practice, voluntary resignation in authorized administrative workflow.
Under formal rules, deployment after retirement under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: PSR 180124 defines transfer as movement of an officer from one post to another of equivalent grade and status.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 180125, the phrase 'head of government department' refers to?
options: The senior-most officer in a department responsible for administration.
Any officer at Grade Level 10 and above.
questions.explanation: PSR 180125 defines 'Head of Government Department' as the senior-most officer responsible for departmental administration and efficiency.

questions.question: Under PSR 180126, what is posting?
options: Assignment of an officer to a new duty station or position.
questions.explanation: PSR 180126 defines posting as the formal assignment of an officer to a new duty station or position within the Service. The correct option states that assignment meaning directly.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 180127, the term 'seniority' refers to?
options: Length of service in a grade relative to others.
questions.explanation: PSR 180127 defines seniority as the relative length of service in a grade level compared with other officers.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180128 provides that 'temporary transfer' is defined as?
options: A transfer for a limited period subject to return.
questions.explanation: PSR 180128 defines temporary transfer as a redeployment of an officer for a limited period after which the officer returns to the parent Ministry.

questions.question: Under PSR 180129, what is an acting appointment?
options: Temporary assignment to perform duties of a higher office.
questions.explanation: PSR 180129 defines an acting appointment as a temporary assignment to perform the duties of a higher office pending substantive appointment. The correct option captures that temporary higher-duty arrangement.

questions.question: Under PSR 180130, what is leave?
options: Temporary absence from duty officially approved by authority.
questions.explanation: PSR 180130 defines leave as a temporary absence from duty that has been officially approved for a stated period. The correct option therefore describes approved temporary absence.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180131 provides that 'efficiency' is defined as?
options: Ability to complete duties with diligence and accuracy.
questions.explanation: PSR 180131 defines efficiency as the ability of an officer to perform assigned duties effectively, promptly, and accurately.

questions.question: According to PSR 180132, what does 'Retirement' refer to?
options: Cessation of service after attainment of statutory age or years of service.
questions.explanation: PSR 180132 defines retirement as cessation of service after attaining the statutory age or prescribed years of service.

questions.question: Under PSR 180133, what does 'Discipline' mean?
options: Control exercised over conduct to maintain order and efficiency.
questions.explanation: PSR 180133 defines discipline as the control exercised over conduct in order to maintain order and efficiency in service.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180134 states that 'dismissal' is defined as?
options: Removal from service as a disciplinary measure without benefits.
questions.explanation: PSR 180134 defines dismissal as removal from the Service as a disciplinary measure with forfeiture of all benefits.

questions.question: Under PSR 180135, what is termination?
options: Disengagement of an officer for reasons other than discipline.
questions.explanation: PSR 180135 defines termination as the disengagement of an officer for reasons other than discipline. The correct option reflects that administrative, non-disciplinary end of appointment.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 180136, 'probationer' is defined as?
options: An officer serving a trial period before confirmation.
questions.explanation: PSR 180136 defines a probationer as an officer serving a trial period before confirmation.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 180137 defines 'temporary staff' as?
options: Officers appointed for a limited period pending confirmation or replacement.
Procedurally, permanent workers within applicable policy conditions.
In practice, interns only in authorized administrative workflow.
Under formal rules, retired officers under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: PSR 180137 defines temporary staff as officers employed for a limited duration pending confirmation or replacement.

questions.question: Under PSR 180138, what do 'Emoluments' mean?
options: Total monetary and non-monetary benefits payable to an officer.
questions.explanation: PSR 180138 defines emoluments as the total monetary and non-monetary benefits payable to an officer.

questions.question: Under PSR 180139, who maintains the seniority list?
options: Each ministry or department under the supervision of the Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 180139 provides that each ministry or department maintains the seniority list under the supervision of the Commission.

questions.question: Under PSR 180140, what is an establishment circular?
options: An official directive issued by the Office of the Head of Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 180140 describes an establishment circular as an official directive issued by the Office of the Head of Civil Service of the Federation. The correct option states that official-directive meaning.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to the regulations, **psr** 190101, the repeal of the 2008 **psr** is defined as?
options: The 2008 edition ceases to have effect.
questions.explanation: PSR 190101 repeals the 2008 edition, meaning only the 2021 edition governs all matters from its commencement date.

questions.question: What happens to disciplinary cases pending before the 2021 Rules under PSR 190102?
options: Continuation under the old Rules until conclusion.
Automatic reopening under the new Rules.
questions.explanation: PSR 190102 provides that disciplinary cases pending before the commencement of the 2021 Rules continue under the old Rules until they are concluded.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 190103 provides that appointments made before 2021 shall?
options: Be reconfirmed under the new PSR.
Remain valid as if made under the 2021 PSR.
questions.explanation: PSR 190103 validates all appointments and actions taken under the repealed Rules as if made under the 2021 edition.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **psr** 190104, the interpretation of transitional provisions rests with?
options: The Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: PSR 190104 places interpretation of transitional and commencement provisions under the authority of the Head of Civil Service.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **psr** 190105 directs that references to repealed provisions in other laws shall be construed as?
options: References to provisions withdrawn without replacement under PSR 190104.
References to the corresponding provisions contained in PSR 2021.
References to prior circulars pending harmonization under PSR 190106.
References requiring case-by-case approval from FCSC under PSR 010103.
questions.explanation: PSR 190105 provides that any legal reference to repealed PSR sections shall now refer to equivalent provisions in PSR 2021.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the according to **psr** 190106, the short title of these rules shall be?
questions.explanation: PSR 190106 provides that the Rules shall be cited as the Public Service Rules 2021.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the **psr** 190107 states that these rules supersede?
options: All previous editions and inconsistent circulars.
questions.explanation: PSR 190107 states that the 2021 Rules supersede all prior editions and circulars inconsistent with their provisions.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the according to **psr** 190109, any circular inconsistent with **psr** 2021 shall?
options: Be regarded as void to the extent of inconsistency.
questions.explanation: PSR 190109 invalidates any circular that contradicts the provisions of the 2021 Public Service Rules.

questions.question: What is the main the overall purpose of chapters 18 and 19 of **psr** 2021 is to?
options: Clarify definitions, establish commencement, and ensure smooth transition.
Procedurally, replace constitutional provisions within applicable policy conditions.
In practice, handle staff promotions in authorized administrative workflow.
Under formal rules, regulate financial disbursements under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: Chapters 18 and 19 aim to provide clear definitions, establish commencement authority, and ensure a seamless transition from previous PSR editions.

questions.question: Which practice best demonstrates sound interpretation and commencement governance under the PSR?
options: Approved interpretation procedure and a complete record trail.
Inconsistent rule use based on personal preference.
Convenience ahead of policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Interpretation and commencement issues should be handled through approved procedure and a complete record trail so the basis of each decision remains lawful and reviewable.

questions.question: Which approach best supports compliance in interpretation and commencement matters?
options: Lawful criteria and transparent documentation of each decision step.
Delayed decisions until avoidable crises emerge.
Routine treatment of exceptions without justification.
Case closure without verifying facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Compliance in interpretation and commencement matters depends on lawful criteria and transparent documentation of each decision step.

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk management in interpretation and commencement work?
options: Early identification of control gaps and prompt escalation of material exceptions.
Skipped review checks in order to save time.
Convenience ahead of policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Risk management in interpretation and commencement work depends on early identification of control gaps and prompt escalation of material exceptions.

questions.question: Which practice reflects proper appointment-governance standards in interpretation and commencement matters?
options: Merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Case closure before validating facts or records.
questions.explanation: Appointment-governance standards are best reflected by merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels supported by the record.

questions.question: Which practice best supports disciplinary process in interpretation and commencement administration?
options: Due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Convenience ahead of policy and legal requirements.
Inconsistent rule application based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Disciplinary process should rest on due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions so the outcome can be defended under the Rules.

questions.question: Which practice best demonstrates proper leave administration in interpretation and commencement work?
options: Consistent leave-rule application and updated personnel records.
Case closure without verifying facts or required records.
Delayed decisions until avoidable crises emerge.
questions.explanation: Leave administration is best demonstrated by consistent application of the leave rules together with updated personnel records.

questions.question: Which approach best supports promotion standards in interpretation and commencement administration?
options: Eligibility confirmation before any recommendation for advancement.
Inconsistent rule application based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards are protected when eligibility is confirmed before any recommendation for advancement is made.

questions.question: Which practice best supports circular compliance in interpretation and commencement work?
options: Alignment with current circular directives and PSR requirements.
Delayed decisions until avoidable crises emerge.
Routine treatment of exceptions without justification.
questions.explanation: Circular compliance requires practice to remain aligned with the current circular directives together with the governing PSR provisions.

questions.question: Which practice reflects proper documented procedure in interpretation and commencement matters?
options: Inconsistent rule application based on personal preference.
Convenience ahead of policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Proper documented procedure in interpretation and commencement matters requires complete records and adherence to the documented process.

questions.question: Which practice best supports compliance assurance in interpretation and commencement work?
options: Consistent application of approved rules and escalation of exceptions.
Delayed decisions until avoidable crises emerge.
Routine treatment of exceptions without justification.
Case closure without verifying facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Compliance assurance is strengthened by consistent application of approved rules together with prompt escalation of exceptions.

questions.question: Which practice best demonstrates public accountability in interpretation and commencement work?
options: Skipped review checks in order to save time.
Convenience ahead of policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Public accountability in interpretation and commencement work depends on traceable decisions supported by evidence-based justification.

questions.question: Which approach best supports service integrity in interpretation and commencement administration?
options: Conflict-of-interest avoidance and disclosure of relevant constraints.
Routine treatment of exceptions without justification.
Case closure without verifying facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Service integrity is protected when conflicts of interest are avoided and relevant constraints are disclosed on the record.

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk control in interpretation and commencement administration?
options: Review of sensitive steps and timely escalation of exceptions.
Skipped control checks because the matter looks routine.
Weak control gaps left unresolved until complaint.
questions.explanation: Risk control in interpretation and commencement work requires sensitive steps to be reviewed and exceptions to be escalated in time.

questions.question: When handling interpretation & commencement, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards?
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in interpretation & commencement Reference: Interpretation & Commencement - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports operational discipline in interpretation and commencement work?
options: Consistent rule application and proper recording of departures.
Repeated non-compliance where output appears satisfactory.
Ad hoc personal judgment in place of procedure.
questions.explanation: Operational discipline is sustained when rules are applied consistently and any departures are properly recorded and reviewed.

questions.question: In the context of Interpretation & Commencement, which action best demonstrates citizen-focused service
options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in interpretation & commencement Reference: Interpretation & Commencement - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which approach best supports record management in interpretation and commencement administration?
options: Complete files and prompt recording of material actions.
Key decisions kept outside the official file for convenience.
Removal of papers without authority or traceability.
Dependence on memory instead of file references in urgent work.
questions.explanation: Record management in interpretation and commencement administration depends on complete files and prompt recording of material actions.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on performance standards within Interpretation & Commencement?
options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in interpretation & commencement Reference: Interpretation & Commencement - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice reflects proper governance standards in interpretation and commencement administration?
options: Approved process, complete documentation, and lawful review channels.
Action first and file reconstruction later if challenged.
Verbal authority treated as enough for every case.
Mandatory checks dropped because the matter seems straightforward.
questions.explanation: Proper governance standards in interpretation and commencement administration require approved process, complete documentation, and lawful review channels.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain interpretation & commencement compliance in Interpretation & Commencement?
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in interpretation & commencement Reference: Interpretation & Commencement - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Interpretation & Commencement, which action best demonstrates interpretation & commencement risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in interpretation & commencement Reference: Interpretation & Commencement - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective interpretation & commencement, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure appointment governance
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in interpretation & commencement Reference: Interpretation & Commencement - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on disciplinary process within Interpretation & Commencement?
options: Due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: A sound disciplinary process depends on due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions that can withstand review.

questions.question: When handling interpretation & commencement, which choice reflects proper leave administration standards?
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in interpretation & commencement Reference: Interpretation & Commencement - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain promotion standards in Interpretation & Commencement?
options: Inconsistent rule application across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Promotion standards remain credible when eligibility is confirmed before advancement is recommended.

questions.question: A unit handling interpretation and commencement faces competing priorities. Which action best preserves compliance and service quality?
options: Skipped review checkpoints under timeline pressure.
Convenience ahead of approved process requirements.
Approved procedures applied consistently with each material step documented.
questions.explanation: Where priorities compete, compliance and service quality are protected by consistent use of approved procedure together with documentation of each material step.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the the provisions of the public service rules (psr) shall be enforced by?
options: All Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices and Agencies
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as All Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices and Agencies, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the the public service rules apply to?
options: Only officers in the Federal Civil Service
All officers in the Public Service
All civil servants except where they conflict with specific terms in their contract
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as All officers in the Public Service, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following offices is NOT explicitly mentioned in the rules?
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Chief Justice of Nigeria, so that option is correct.

questions.question: To what extent do these rules apply to the offices mentioned in Rule 010102?
options: Application in every respect without limitation.
Application only where no inconsistency with the Constitution exists.
Application only at the discretion of the officers concerned.
questions.explanation: Rule 010102 provides that the rules apply to the listed offices only to the extent that they are not inconsistent with the Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the duty of every officer regarding the Public Service Rules" according to official standards?
options: To be aware of some of the rules
To acquaint himself with the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars
To seek clarification from their superiors
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To acquaint himself with the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars).

questions.question: Where are the special meanings of words and terms used in particular chapters or sections found?
options: At the end of the document
At the beginning of such chapters or sections
In the Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 defines the term as At the beginning of such chapters or sections, which is why that option is correct.

questions.question: What is the official definition of "Accredited Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria" according to the relevant regulations?
options: The Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria within the area
Any representative of the Nigerian government
A representative accredited to the United Nations
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as The Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria within the area, so that option is correct.

questions.question: What is the official definition of "Civil Service of the Federation" according to the relevant regulations?
options: Service in any government office at any level.
Service of the Federation in a civil capacity in the offices and ministries of the Federal Government, enjoying continuity of existence.
Service in the armed forces of the Federation.
Contract service rendered to the Federal Government.
questions.explanation: The Civil Service of the Federation means service of the Federation in a civil capacity in the offices and ministries assigned responsibility for Federal Government business, with continuity of existence.

questions.question: What is the official definition of "Cut-off Mark" according to the relevant regulations?
options: The lowest score on an examination
Examination score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Examination score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies, so that option is correct.

questions.question: What is an "Established Post"?
options: A post provided for under the Personnel Emoluments sub-head of the Estimates.
A post in any of the Public Service of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as A post provided for under the Personnel Emoluments sub-head of the Estimates, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who is an "Expatriate Officer"?
options: An officer from another African country
A staff who ranks as such for the purpose of pension in accordance with the Pension Reform Act 2004 as amended.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (A staff who ranks as such for the purpose of pension in accordance with the Pension Reform Act 2004 as amended.).

questions.question: What is the official definition of "Gazette" according to the relevant regulations?
options: The Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as The Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who is a "Junior Officer"?
options: An officer on GL.08 and below
A pensionable officer on GL.06 and below and their equivalent.
Any officer below the rank of director
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as A pensionable officer on GL.06 and below and their equivalent, so that option is correct.

questions.question: What is the official definition of "Monthly-Rated" according to the relevant regulations?
options: Employment on monthly rates of pay.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Employment on monthly rates of pay, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the term "Nigerian Home Place" as used in the Public Service Rules?
options: The place in Nigeria accepted in writing by Government as the place to which a staff would naturally wish to return when spending his leave in Nigeria.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as The place in Nigeria accepted in writing by Government as the place to which a staff would naturally wish to return when spending his leave in Nigeria, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who is a "Nigerian Officer"?
options: An officer other than an expatriate.
An officer from a specific region
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as An officer other than an expatriate, so that option is correct.

questions.question: What does "Officer" mean when used without qualification?
options: Staff in an established post, either on pensionable or contract terms.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Staff in an established post, either on pensionable or contract terms.).

questions.question: What is the official definition of "Pass Mark" according to the relevant regulations?
options: Minimum score required for initial employment.
Average score recorded in the examination.
Score below the passing threshold for the promotion examination, currently 60 percent.
Highest score obtained in the examination.
questions.explanation: Pass mark means the score below which a candidate is regarded as having failed the promotion examination; under the current rule, that threshold is 60%.

questions.question: Who is a "Permanent Secretary" under the Rules?
options: Officer in charge of day-to-day administration in a ministry or extra-ministerial office, serving as accounting officer and chief policy adviser to the Minister.
General administrative officer in a department.
questions.explanation: A Permanent Secretary is the officer in charge of day-to-day administration in a ministry or extra-ministerial office and also serves as accounting officer and chief policy adviser to the Minister.

questions.question: What is the official definition of "Public Service of the Federation" according to the relevant regulations?
options: The service of the Federation in any capacity in respect of the Government of the Federation as defined in part 4 section 318 of 1999 Constitution (as amended).
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The service of the Federation in any capacity in respect of the Government of the Federation as defined in part 4 section 318 of 1999 Constitution (as amended).).

questions.question: What do "Public Service Rules" consist of?
options: Policies and guidelines which define the employment relationship between Public Servants and the Government; they also spell out the Conditions of Service and Human Resources Management procedures.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Policies and guidelines which define the employment relationship between Public Servants and the Government; they also spell out the Conditions of Service and Human Resources Management procedures, so that option is correct.

questions.question: What is a "Scheme of Service"?
options: A welfare package for public servants

A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.).

questions.question: Who is a "Spouse of an Officer"?
options: A wife/husband married under the Marriage Act, Customary Law or Islamic Law.
Only a wife married under the Marriage Act
Only a husband married under Islamic Law
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A wife/husband married under the Marriage Act, Customary Law or Islamic Law.).

questions.question: What is the official definition of "Staff" according to the relevant regulations?
options: A person employed by the Federal Government.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as A person employed by the Federal Government, so that option is correct.

questions.question: What is the official definition of "Suspension" according to the relevant regulations?
options: Removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office, where a prima facie case, of a serious nature has been established against the Officer, during which period he shall earn no salary.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office, where a prima facie case, of a serious nature has been established against the Officer, during which period he shall earn no salary.).

questions.question: Who is a "Trainee"?
options: Person appointed to a training post in any grade, including a pupil.
questions.explanation: A trainee is a person appointed to a training post in any grade, and the term includes a pupil.

questions.question: How can the Federal Government amend the provisions of the Public Service Rules?
options: By issuing an appropriate Circular during the lifespan of a PSR.
Through the Head of Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (By issuing an appropriate Circular during the lifespan of a PSR.).

questions.question: How are the terms 'Officer' and 'Staff' referred to in the Public Service Rules to be applied in terms of gender?
options: The provisions of the Rules apply equally to both genders.
At the discretion of the Permanent Secretary
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The provisions of the Rules apply equally to both genders.).

questions.question: What should a copy of the Public Service Rules form part of?
options: The inventory of items in all offices of the Public Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as The inventory of items in all offices of the Public Service, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the appointments to offices in the federal civil service are made on the authority of?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as The Federal Civil Service Commission, so that option is correct.

questions.question: How are appointments made?
options: Either by letter written under the direction of the Federal Civil Service Commission or by formal agreement.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Either by letter written under the direction of the Federal Civil Service Commission or by formal agreement, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who is authorized to appoint eligible candidates to posts in respect of which the powers of appointment have been delegated to them?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the federal civil service commission shall make appointments to posts graded?
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as GL.07-17, so that option is correct.

questions.question: How shall appointments by the FCSC be made?
options: As the need arises into the available vacancies after advertisement.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as As the need arises into the available vacancies after advertisement, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Where shall advertisement for vacancies be placed?
options: In three national newspapers and the Commission's website.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as In three national newspapers and the Commission's website, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the deadline for submission of applications from interested candidates" according to official standards?
options: Six weeks from the date of placement of the advertisement.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Six weeks from the date of placement of the advertisement, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who shall select from the pool of successful candidates at the Civil Service Examination?
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Each Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who handles the selection process in each Ministry?
options: The appropriate Committees of each Ministry with representatives of the FCSC and OHCSF.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as The appropriate Committees of each Ministry with representatives of the FCSC and OHCSF, so that option is correct.

questions.question: How often is the competitive Public/Civil Service Entry Examination held for posts graded GL. 07-10?
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Annually, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who conducts the Public/Civil Service Entry Examination?
options: FCSC in conjunction with OHCSF, ASCON and PSIN.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as FCSC in conjunction with OHCSF, ASCON and PSIN, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the qualification requirement for the Public/Civil Service Entry Examination" according to official standards?
options: As provided for in the approved Schemes of Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as As provided for in the approved Schemes of Service, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who makes the final recommendation for appointments?
options: The line Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices in line with their requirements.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as The line Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices in line with their requirements, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who appoints Junior Staff on GL. 06 and below?
options: Junior Staff Committee of each Ministry/Office.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Junior Staff Committee of each Ministry/Office, so that option is correct.

questions.question: How are appointments in Federal Offices located in the States/Zones made?
options: From the residents of those States/Zones who possess the qualifications.
From other states if no local candidates qualify
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (From the residents of those States/Zones who possess the qualifications.).

questions.question: Who works out the actual establishment for each State Office?
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as OHCSF, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Who conducts the interview to appoint the Officers (Junior Staff)?
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Junior Staff Committee (Local), so that option is correct.

questions.question: When is the incremental date of an Officer appointed or promoted to a post?
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as 1st January of every year, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the condition for an Officer to enjoy the increment" according to official standards?
options: Spending at least six (6) months on the Grade with good performance.
questions.explanation: PSR 000000 states the correct interpretation as Spending at least six (6) months on the Grade with good performance, so that option is correct.

questions.question: Is the employment of unpaid staff allowed?
options: Allowance with approval of the Head of Service.
Prohibition of the employment of unpaid staff.
