questions.explanation: Traceability and fairness improve when personnel and performance decisions are documented and checked against the rules.

questions.question: For sustainable results in Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms, which practice should be prioritized first?
options: Consistent application of PSR provisions with auditable records.
questions.explanation: Sustainable results come from applying the same PSR provisions consistently and keeping the supporting record auditable.

questions.question: In a time-sensitive matter under circulars: personnel, performance & reforms, which action best aligns with lawful administrative standards' (Rule-compliant administration scenario 1)
options: Rely on verbal approval and close the file without documentary trail.
Apply applicable PSR provisions consistently and keep auditable records.
Delay escalation until issues become material and difficult to reverse.
Proceed without validating source records and decision criteria.
questions.explanation: Lawful administrative standards depend on applying the approved rules and keeping a clear documentary trail even when the matter is urgent.

questions.question: The fees for invigilators and examiners are for:?
options: All examinations conducted in the public service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Only the compulsory confirmation examinations.). This section is specifically titled "Fees for Invigilators and Examiners" under the chapter on "Prescribed Examination for Confirmation," indicating its scope.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: What determines the seniority of an officer?
options: The earlier certified date between appointment and assumption of duty.
The date of assumption of duty only.
questions.explanation: Rule 020105 states that seniority is determined by the date of assumption of duty certified by the appropriate officer and/or the date of present appointment as reflected in the appropriate register, whichever is earlier.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: What may happen if a clerical grade officer fails the compulsory confirmation examination during probation?
options: The officer will be retired immediately.
The appointment may be terminated at the end of the probationary period.
The officer will be confirmed automatically.
The officer will be transferred to another cadre without assessment.
questions.explanation: For clerical grade officers, failure to pass the compulsory confirmation examination during probation may lead to termination of the appointment at the end of the probationary period.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Responsibility for serves as resource persons during the selection workflow for permanent secretaries belongs to which role?
options: The Civil/Public Service and Private institutions.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Federal Civil Service Commission. a
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Civil/Public Service and Private institutions.). Rule 020813 states that "Resource persons from the Civil/Public Service and Private Institutions may be involved in the selection procedure." Part 11: Leaving the Service
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: What must a clerical grade officer secure by passing the compulsory confirmation examination?
questions.explanation: Passing the compulsory confirmation examination is part of the probation requirement that leads to confirmation of appointment.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: For a probationary officer, how often should the compulsory confirmation examinations be held?
options: As stipulated by the Examination Board Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Once every six months). Rule 030106 specifies that these examinations must be held at intervals of not more than six months.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which option professional officers are exempted from passing the compulsory confirmation examination?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Law professionals □). Rule 030109 grants an exemption from the compulsory confirmation examination to law professionals.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Select the statement that materials are allowed to be used by candidates during the compulsory confirmation examinations?
options: Reference books specified by the Examination Board.
All Public Service Rules and Financial Regulations.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Reference books specified by the Examination Board.). Rule 030108 specifies that candidates may be allowed to use specified reference books during the examinations.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: The consequence for an officer who fails to present themselves for a compulsory confirmation examination without an acceptable reason?
options: The officer is given another chance to write the exam.
The officer's appointment is liable to be terminated.
The officer is suspended for a month.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The officer's appointment is liable to be terminated.). According to Rule 030105, an officer who fails to present himself/herself for the examination without an acceptable reason is liable to have their appointment terminated. Part 16: Compulsory Confirmation Examinations for Senior Executive Officers, Administrative Officers and Professionals (COMPRO I & II)
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: An officer on GL 11 is required to sit for which compulsory confirmation examination?
options: Both COMPRO I and COMPRO II.
questions.explanation: COMPRO II applies to officers on Grade Levels 10 to 14, so an officer on GL 11 is required to sit for COMPRO II.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: How many sittings is an officer allowed for the compulsory confirmation examination?
questions.explanation: The rule for the compulsory confirmation examination allows an officer a maximum of three sittings. The item therefore tests the permitted number of attempts, not the definition of the examination itself.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which body prepares the syllabus for compulsory confirmation examinations for junior officers?
options: Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation prepares the syllabus for the compulsory confirmation examinations for junior officers.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: The effect of failing the confirmation examination for a paramilitary officer on probation?
options: They are transferred to another unit.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Their appointment is terminated.). The general rule (030104) on failing confirmation examinations applies here, leading to termination of appointment
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Are officers in the police and paramilitary services required to sit for the civil-service COMPRO I or COMPRO II examinations?
options: Requirement for both COMPRO I and COMPRO II.
Use of examinations prescribed by the relevant service commission.
Optional examination status across every service.
questions.explanation: Rule 030401 provides that officers in the police and paramilitary services take the examinations prescribed by their own service commissions rather than the civil-service COMPRO examinations.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Approve to approve the fees for invigilators and examiners for the compulsory confirmation examinations?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) 56 Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) 56 Q). Rule 030601 states that the examination fees must be determined by the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Responsible for establishing and supervising Federal payment Offices in each state capital of the federation?
questions.explanation: The Accountant-General is responsible for establishing and supervising Federal payment offices in each state capital under the Financial Regulations.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who is personally responsible for ensuring that expenditure against a sub-head affected by a virement warrant does not exceed the reduced balance?
options: Officers authorized to incur expenditure (officers controlling votes).
The Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 317 places personal responsibility on officers controlling votes to ensure expenditure does not exceed the reduced balance after a virement.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: If a serious irregularity in a voucher is established or suspected, what is the first action the officer controlling the vote should take?
options: Immediately investigate and report the circumstances.
Report to the Minister of Finance.
Transfer the amount to a suspense account.
questions.explanation: Where a serious irregularity in a voucher is established or suspected, the officer controlling the vote must immediately investigate and report the circumstances.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: What happens when expenditure is incorrectly charged to a vote?
options: It will be transferred to a general fund.
It will be adjusted in the next financial year.
It will be reported to the Minister of Finance for approval.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 417 states that expenditure incorrectly charged to a vote must be disallowed.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which office is responsible for the appointment of the Internal Auditor?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1702 provides that the Accountant-General is responsible for the appointment and deployment of Internal Auditors.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: The existence of "ghost workers" is usually a sign of what problem in the Civil Service?
options: Inadequate linkages between training and duty posts.
Poor personnel records and a flawed payroll control system.
questions.explanation: The existence of "ghost workers" usually points to poor personnel records and a weak payroll control system, especially where staff records are not properly integrated and verified.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which two key institutions have oversight responsibilities for policy articulation and implementation, as well as quality assurance on Human Resources issues?
options: The National Council on Establishments (NCE) and the National Planning Commission, a.
The National Assembly and the Judiciary.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) and the Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The Ministry of Finance and the Budget Office of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) and the Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).). The key institutions with oversight responsibilities for HR issues are the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) and the Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which role is responsible for promoting good relations between Ministers and other political appointees, on the one hand, and Permanent Secretaries and other Civil Servants on the other hand?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.). One of the functions of the OHCSF is promoting good relations between Ministers and other political appointees, on the one hand, and Permanent Secretaries and other Civil Servants on the other hand.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to the Civil Service Handbook, what is a chief element in good governance?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Service delivery). The handbook states that service delivery is a chief element in good governance, and efficient and effective delivery of good service is the raison d'etre of government.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Personnel, Performance & Reforms - Expansion Set

questions.question: Why did the Public Service Rules incorporate provisions for virtual meetings?
options: To align public officers with party politics.
To support continuity of official work during emergencies and remote operations.
To increase ministerial control over routine workflow.
To make official travel reduction the only meeting policy.
questions.explanation: The Public Service Rules incorporated virtual-meeting provisions to support continuity of official work during emergencies and remote operating conditions.
